Whether you're a seasoned tequila lover or new to the spirit, the bartenders at The Syndicate will craft the perfect drink for you. From classic margaritas to inventive tequila-based cocktails, there’s something for everyone to enjoy.

Flying Pepper Cantina

Flying Pepper Cantina is another fantastic spot to celebrate National Tequila Day. Known for its vibrant ambiance and delicious drinks, this bar offers a variety of tequila options that are sure to delight your taste buds. Whether you’re in the mood for a refreshing margarita, a tequila sunrise, or something more adventurous, Flying Pepper Cantina has you covered. It’s the perfect place to relax, enjoy great company, and toast to this special day.

Other Downtown Favorites

While The Syndicate and Flying Pepper Cantina are top choices for tequila lovers, don’t forget to explore other bars in Downtown Bellefontaine that might have hidden gems on their drink menus. You never know where you might find your new favorite tequila cocktail!

Fun Facts About Tequila

To make your National Tequila Day celebration even more enjoyable, here are some fun facts about this iconic spirit:

  • Agave Plant: Tequila is made from the blue agave plant, primarily grown in the region surrounding the city of Tequila in Mexico. The heart of the plant, called the piña, is harvested and roasted to extract the sugars needed for fermentation.
  • Types of Tequila: There are several types of tequila, each with its unique flavor profile. Blanco (or silver) tequila is unaged and has a crisp, clean taste. Reposado is aged for at least two months but less than a year in oak barrels, giving it a smooth, slightly woody flavor. Añejo is aged for at least one year but less than three, resulting in a rich, complex taste. Extra Añejo is aged for over three years and offers a deep, luxurious flavor.
  • Tequila vs. Mezcal: While all tequilas are mezcals, not all mezcals are tequilas. Tequila is a type of mezcal made specifically from blue agave and produced in designated regions of Mexico. Mezcal can be made from various types of agave and often has a smokier flavor due to the production process.
  • Tequila Sunrise: The tequila sunrise is a classic cocktail made with tequila, orange juice, and grenadine. Its vibrant colors mimic a beautiful sunrise, making it a popular choice for celebratory occasions.
  • Responsible Drinking: Tequila, like all alcoholic beverages, should be enjoyed responsibly. Remember to pace yourself, stay hydrated, and never drink and drive. Celebrating responsibly ensures that everyone can have a great time safely.

Join the Celebration!

National Tequila Day on Wednesday, July 24th, is the perfect opportunity to gather with friends and family, enjoy delicious tequila cocktails, and create lasting memories. Head to Downtown Bellefontaine and explore The Syndicate, Flying Pepper Cantina, and other local bars to discover the best tequila offerings in town. Cheers to good times, great drinks, and celebrating responsibly!

item.title: Sip, Stroll, and Savor: Art, Wine & Jazz Fest in Downtown Bellefontaine
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
This Saturday, September 7th, from 2 to 5 PM, the heart of Downtown Bellefontaine will come alive with the vibrant sounds of jazz, the rich aromas of wine, and the captivating beauty of local art. The Downtown Bellefontaine Partnership is proud to present the Art, Wine & Jazz - Second Line Fest, an event that promises to be an unforgettable afternoon of culture, flavor, and celebration. Sponsored by The Steinhelfer Firm and Minnich Law Offices, this ticketed event offers a unique opportunity to explore the community while indulging in global wine selections and enjoying live entertainment.

Discover the World of Wine

At the Art, Wine & Jazz Fest, wine lovers will have the chance to embark on a tasting journey featuring wines from all corners of the globe. Whether you prefer a crisp sauvignon blanc from New Zealand, a bold cabernet sauvignon from California, or a rich Malbec from Argentina, this event has something for every palate. The wine tasting experience is expertly curated by Bella Vino Events & Wine Room, ensuring that each pour highlights the diversity and richness of wines from renowned wine-producing regions around the world.

Your ticket grants you access to sample 10 wines of your choice, and tastings will take place at various downtown businesses. As you stroll from one location to another, you’ll have the chance to explore local shops, meet fellow wine enthusiasts, and enjoy the lively atmosphere of Downtown Bellefontaine.

Live Jazz & Street Performances

Setting the mood for the day will be a local jazz band performing throughout the event. Their soulful melodies will fill the air as you sip and savor each wine selection, creating the perfect backdrop for a relaxing and sophisticated afternoon. Additionally, street musician Pete Moss will be performing live, adding his easy-listening vibes to the mix. The combination of jazz and street music will make this event feel like a mini New Orleans festival right in the heart of Bellefontaine.

Join the Second Line Parade

One of the most exciting aspects of this year’s fest is the addition of a second line parade. A second line parade is a lively tradition rooted in New Orleans culture, often seen at jazz funerals and community celebrations. Participants follow a brass band through the streets, dancing and waving handkerchiefs or parasols, celebrating the spirit of the occasion. At the Art, Wine & Jazz Fest, you’ll have the chance to join in the fun as the second line winds through the streets of downtown, following the jazz performance. It’s the perfect way to wrap up the afternoon, bringing a festive, upbeat close to the event.

Get Your Tickets Today

Tickets for the Art, Wine & Jazz Fest are on sale now. For just $25, you’ll receive 10 wine tastings, or you can purchase two tickets for $45. This is an afternoon you won’t want to miss, so be sure to secure your tickets in advance through Eventbrite here.

Don’t miss the chance to enjoy exquisite wines, soulful jazz, and the vibrant community spirit of Downtown Bellefontaine this Saturday. Grab your tickets, gather your friends, and get ready to sip, stroll, and savor everything this unique event has to offer!

item.title: 8 Things to Do This Christmas in Downtown Bellefontaine
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Christmas in Downtown Bellefontaine is full of charm and holiday magic! Whether you’re shopping for the perfect gift, grabbing a festive drink, or enjoying seasonal events, our downtown has something for everyone. Here are 8 must-do activities to make your Christmas unforgettable:

1. Shop at Our Best-in-State Retail Stores

Downtown Bellefontaine is home to some of Ohio’s best shopping. From gourmet chocolates at Queen of Hearts Fine Chocolates & Gifts to unique finds at The Fun Company and Nest 1896, you’ll discover plenty of gifts for everyone on your list. Whether you're looking for handcrafted chocolates, stylish home décor, or one-of-a-kind toys, these shops offer something special for your holiday shopping.

2. Watch the Hometown Christmas Parade

Kick off your holiday season with the annual Hometown Christmas Parade on Friday, December 6. This beloved tradition lights up downtown with festive floats, marching bands, and, of course, Santa Claus! It’s the perfect way to get into the holiday spirit with friends and family.

3. Sip on Delicious Holiday Drinks

Warm up this season with special holiday drinks from downtown’s best spots. Cozy up with a peppermint mocha from Sweet Aromas or a seasonal latte from Native Coffee. If you’re in the mood for something stronger, head to The Syndicate or Six Hundred Downtown for creative holiday cocktails that are sure to make your spirits bright.

4. Take a Selfie at a Holiday Selfie Station

Make lasting memories at one of our festive downtown selfie stations! Snap a picture with your loved ones at the Mistletoe Avenue kissing booth at Rainbow Row Bellefontaine or strike a pose with the Christmas Jeep on the Six Hundred Downtown patio. These charming setups are perfect for capturing the magic of the season.

5. Dine at a Local Restaurant

Enjoy a holiday meal at one of Downtown Bellefontaine’s fantastic restaurants. Grab a craft beer and gourmet burger at Brewfontaine, savor a classic diner experience at Don's Diner, or enjoy Mexican-inspired flavors at Flying Pepper Cantina. Whether you're grabbing a quick bite or enjoying a holiday dinner, downtown’s dining scene has something for everyone.

6. Catch a Holiday Show at the Holland Theatre

The historic Holland Theatre is your go-to for holiday entertainment. On Sunday, December 1, enjoy Madcap Puppets: The Nutcracker. On Saturday, December 7, and Sunday, December 8, experience A Swingin' Christmas by Windmill Productions. For a musical treat, don’t miss the Lima Symphony Orchestra Christmas show on Sunday, December 15. These shows are perfect for creating holiday memories with the family.

7. Enter the Win the Window Contest

From November 22 to December 18, enter the Win the Window contest for a chance to win Downtown Bellefontaine gifts worth over $1,000! Shop at participating businesses, get your card stamped, and you could take home an incredible holiday prize bundle. This is your chance to support local shops and possibly win big this Christmas.

8. Get Your Photo with Santa at the Santa Shed

No holiday season is complete without a visit to Santa! Stop by the FREE Santa Shed, presented by the Downtown Bellefontaine Partnership, on Saturdays, Sundays, and select Fridays throughout the Christmas season. Bring the whole family to meet Santa and capture a festive photo to treasure forever. Check out everything there is to do on the Downtown Bellefontaine website. This holiday season, make Downtown Bellefontaine your destination for shopping, dining, and unforgettable memories. We can’t wait to celebrate with you!

item.title: Introducing Ohio’s Christmas Capital
item.dc:creator: admin_nexa
item.content:encoded:
Experience the Magic of the Holiday Season in Downtown Bellefontaine

Downtown Bellefontaine Event Coordinator Maddie Stotler, Downtown Bellefontaine Partnership President Andrea Wrocklage, Bellefontaine Mayor David Crissman, Logan County Visitors Bureau Director Anna Louden, Small Nation Founder Jason Duff

November 25, 2024 – Downtown Bellefontaine is officially Ohio’s Christmas Capital, and this season, the historic heart of Bellefontaine is ready to deliver a magical holiday experience. With an enchanting Christmas parade, appearances from Santa every weekend, and some of the best holiday shopping and dining in the state, Downtown Bellefontaine is set to be a top destination for holiday festivities.

“This year, our team has gone above and beyond to make Downtown Bellefontaine a true winter wonderland,” says Andrea Wrocklage, President of the Downtown Bellefontaine Partnership and a longtime downtown retailer. “We’re incredibly proud of the way our small businesses, community organizations, and local talent have come together to make the downtown an experience like no other. This season, we invite everyone to discover the magic here and see why Bellefontaine truly shines during the holidays.”

Bellefontaine Mayor David Crissman proclaims Downtown Bellefontaine as Ohio's Christmas Capital.

This year, downtown’s calendar is packed with holiday cheer. In addition to a charming parade to kick off the season, Santa Claus himself will be stationed downtown each weekend to greet visitors of all ages at the Downtown Bellefontaine Partnership’s Santa Shed. Downtown Bellefontaine also offers unmatched gift shopping, with a curated selection of boutiques, shops, and retailers featuring unique and thoughtful gifts. Restaurants throughout downtown offer warm and inviting spaces to gather and enjoy the flavors of the season. And for those looking to capture the holiday magic, special photo ops are set up throughout the town, perfect for seasonal snapshots and family photos.

Bellefontaine’s historic atmospheric theatre, the Holland Theatre has many holiday-themed shows scheduled for this season, adding live entertainment to the downtown’s festive atmosphere. Guests can enjoy a mix of classic holiday performances and family-friendly productions, creating unforgettable experiences for locals and visitors alike. Other downtown events include cookie decorating classes, family photo sessions, and an ugly sweater bar crawl. For a full list of events and things to do this Christmas season, visit downtownbellefontaine.com/Christmas.

“Bellefontaine’s transformation into Ohio’s Christmas Capital celebrates everything our community loves about this time of year,” says Jason Duff, Founder & CEO of Small Nation. “From the decorated storefronts to the twinkling lights of our new Mistletoe Avenue, there’s nothing like the magic of downtown during the holidays. It’s a place where families and friends can come together to create memories that last a lifetime.”

Bellefontaine Mayor's Proclamation

“This Christmas season, however you celebrate – in church, with family, or with friends – it is my hope that all have a safe and Merry Christmas,” Bellefontaine Mayor David Crissman added.

Visit Downtown Bellefontaine this holiday season to shop, dine, explore Mistletoe Avenue, and discover for yourself why we’re known as Ohio’s Christmas Capital.

item.title: Things to Do in Bellefontaine Ohio After a Day at Mad River Mountain
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Planning a trip to Mad River Mountain and want to round out the day with other activities? Or perhaps you’ve had a blast carving up the slopes, and you’re not quite ready to go home.

Now what?

Bellefontaine is nearby, and you might be surprised by the assortment of activities, restaurants, attractions, and unique shops you'll love. Here’s how to make the most of your time in this charming Logan County town.

Make it the perfect Saturday, Sunday, or any other part of the week! No matter what, you'll be surprised that Bellefontaine is at the center of so much to do.

Start with a Slice of Pizza Heaven

Let's start with restaurants. Hungry for a slice of za? Check out Six Hundred Downtown – it’s a pizza spot that stands out in all the right ways. Each pizza is hand-spun with top-quality local ingredients, and the dough is made fresh in-house.

What makes these pies special is the brick oven where they’re baked at a scorching 600 degrees, giving the crust that perfect crispy finish. (The name "Six Hundred" is a nod to the oven temperature, which gives the pizzas their signature taste!)

And you know you're getting the good stuff with owner Brittany Saxton at the helm. After all, she's a world pizza champion who’s even competed on Guy’s Grocery Games on the Food Network, so you know she knows her pizza. (And you can feel confident you'll love what's set in front of you.)

Grab a Craft Beer at Ohio’s Best Beer Bar

If you’re going to grab a drink, why not go to Ohio's best bar? We mean that literally.

Brewfontaine, located on Main St., has earned the #1 Beer Bar title in Ohio! Not just Bellefontaine, and not just Logan County.

The best in OHIO!

And it’s not just about the drinks, it’s the experiences they offer. For example, their “Paint Your Partner” event is coming up on February 13, just in time for Valentine’s Day. (It's the perfect date to spend with your special person.) And that’s just one example—they have trivia nights, folks have enjoyed a “cocktail class,” while others pile in for tastings, music, and more.

Did we mention the food? Your only problem here will be deciding what to eat between all the selections, whether that’s their Smashburger, Reuben, smoked BLT, or an assortment of other delectable choices!

A Perfect Spot for Sunday Brunch

Check out The Syndicate! Stepping in, you'll notice this old-school charm, with historic touches like local newspapers and glass windows. But it's not completely washed in the past; it has this modern elegance that keeps it feeling fresh, too.

And the food? Dive into the menu, with offerings like mimosa flights, chicken and waffles, shrimp and grits, butternut squash ravioli, salmon and grilled shrimp skewer, steakhouse burger, and much more.

Mexican Delight

If you're craving authentic Mexican food that's both fresh and flavorful, stop at The Flying Pepper Cantina. Founded by Humberto Nieto and Laura Haverkos, this food truck brings the flavors of Mexico straight to Bellefontaine.

Humberto, originally from a small town in Michoacán, Mexico, creates every dish using traditional recipes he learned growing up—nothing you eat is pre-made or processed. Your body will be in Logan County, but your taste buds will be south of the border.

What sets The Flying Pepper apart is Humberto’s passion for cooking with fresh, local ingredients. He’s committed to supporting local farmers; you’ll taste the difference and understand why it gets great reviews.

And hey, a little spice might be more than welcome after time on the icy slopes!

Sweet Treats for the Whole Family

Got a sweet tooth? Want to keep the kids happy and open to not going home quite yet? Candy Craze is a candy store stockpiled with classic treats like Pop Rocks to the Pucker Power Station, where you can make your own candy mix.

Tanisha McCreary, the owner (who traded in accounting for candy!), has packed the shop with local goodies, nostalgic faves, and even sugar-free options. (Hey, those sugar-free ones can assuage a little bit of guilt as you watch your children beam and scream with happiness!)

Toy Story

After skiing or snowboarding at Mad River Mountain, heading into nearby The Fun Company is a great reward for your kids to pick out a toy, and they’ll love the wide selection of games, books, puzzles, and more.

Plus, with their weekly events, there’s always something fun happening, so you can make your Logan County trip even more special. It’s a great way to celebrate how well your young kids have behaved so far, all while supporting a local business!

Work Your Mind in an Escape Room

Still full of energy after hitting the slopes? Head to Decode Zone for a fun and affordable way to keep the excitement going!

With escape rooms like Spooky Forest, where you race against time to break a curse, or Chess Board, a mind-bending twist on classic games, there's something for everyone to enjoy. Solve the mystery of the missing cookie in Spice Cookies, or wrap things up with a mechanic-themed challenge in Mechanic Wrench.

Who knew Bellefontaine offered so much excitement?

Shop Local Gems in Downtown Bellefontaine

Sometimes there’s no better way to unwind than with some shopping, and you’ll be happily surprised by Bellefontaine’s retail scene. After shopping, take a relaxing stroll in Southview Park, which offers walking tracks and picnic areas.

At Queen of Hearts Fine Chocolates & Gifts, you can indulge in gourmet chocolates and find one-of-a-kind gifts, including hand-painted pottery and MacKenzie-Childs pieces. If you’re looking for trendy farmhouse-style décor, Just U’Neeq sells stylish wares to freshen up your home. Mystic Mama Rock + Jewelry offers a stunning selection of jewelry and global treasures, from amethyst trees to semi-precious stones.

For a bit of self-care, head to The Naked Goat Bath & Body, where you’ll find luxurious all-natural bath and body products from top-tier brands and local artisans.

Antiques - You Can't Find These on Amazon!

If antiques are your thing, discover these two must-see spots:

At The Nest 1896, you’ll find a cozy mix of vintage, handmade, and local goodies in the corner of a beautiful old building. With over 55 vendors, it’s a great spot for unique home décor, jewelry, clothing, and more—perfect for anyone looking for something special.

The Olde Mint Antiques is a treasure hunter’s dream. With three floors packed with antiques, books, and quirky finds, it’s easy to get lost in all the hidden gems. The prices are fair, and the friendly vibe makes it a great place to explore.

Catch a Show at The Holland Theatre

Want to sit down and relax—all while being entertained? The Holland Theatre hosts a range of performances, from Broadway-style shows to classic movies. And you'll love drinking in the aesthetics at this unique destination.

You see, this historic building opened way back in 1931. It stands out for its Dutch-themed architecture, which is rare in the U.S. The theater’s interior, resembling a quaint Dutch village, adds to the history and the charm. After seeing the list of shows, you might be planning yet another trip to Bellefontaine, giving the latest story unfolding before your eyes the highest of reviews!

Ohio Caverns

Seeking more adventure after Mad River Mountain? Ohio Caverns is a must-visit attraction for anyone who wants to explore the natural wonders of Logan County. Located just outside of downtown Bellefontaine, this cave system is the largest in the state of Ohio, featuring over two miles of underground passageways and chambers.

In addition to its natural beauty, Ohio Caverns is also a great place to learn about the geology and history of the area!

After Mad River Mountain

After an awesome time on the slopes at Mad River Mountain, why not keep the energy going by spending time in Bellefontaine? This welcoming town, a highlight of the state, will offer you plenty of nice spots to eat, shop, and unwind. And it's also so close to Ohio Caverns. Whether you’re with family and friends, or just exploring the area on your own, Bellefontaine’s charm will make you want to come back again and again.

item.title: Central Ohio Day Trips: Discover Bellefontaine
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Central Ohio day trips are something to look forward to, no doubt. But where to go? You want something memorable, engaging, not too expensive, and fun.

Live in Columbus, Ohio? Dayton? Lima? Bellefontaine is the perfect destination. Just an easy hour’s drive—this charming town is a lovely place that offers incredible food, unique shops, and small-town vibes that make it a great place to visit, whether it's a date experience or a family adventure.

While Central Ohio boasts many attractions, from Cuyahoga Valley National Park to the Columbus Zoo, Bellefontaine is a must-visit for the best day trips.

Why? How? We'll show you!

Why Bellefontaine Beats Other Day Trips

Decisions, decisions. You want to go on a day trip, but where?

Central Ohio is packed with destinations like Hocking Hills, Yellow Springs, and even the Erie Canal Towpath Trail, but Bellefontaine offers a unique blend of culinary delights and cozy charm that’s hard to beat.

Unlike the long treks to Cuyahoga Valley National Park or the Columbus Zoo, Bellefontaine delivers a compact, walkable experience perfect for a single day. It’s a lovely place to find plenty of local flavor without overwhelming crowds, making it one of the best day trips from Columbus, Ohio.

Bellefontaine’s appeal lies in its ability to offer a little bit of everything—food, shopping, history, and art—in a way that feels authentic. Ohio’s role as a hub of small-town charm is perfectly showcased here, where every corner of the town tells a story.

Once you arrive, you'll see what we mean. Step into the various eateries and shops, talk to locals and business owners, and you'll hear all kinds of stories. Impactful, surprising, and more.

And you'll soon discover people love this town. It doesn't matter if you’re strolling down Main Street or exploring a local boutique; you’ll feel the community pride that sets Bellefontaine apart from more tourist-heavy spots.

You'll also feel that personal welcome and a sense of connection that will make you feel both comfortable and intrigued.

The Food Scene: Bellefontaine’s Biggest Draw

Bellefontaine’s restaurants are causing a buzz for a reason.

Here, you’ll discover hidden gem eateries that make every bite memorable. The whole family will love the variety, from craft beer to flavorful Mexican dishes, making it a top pick for the best day trips.

Six Hundred Downtown: Pizza Perfection

Start your culinary adventure at Six Hundred Downtown, a pizza haven that’s a cut above. Each pie is hand-spun with fresh, local ingredients, baked in a 600-degree brick oven for a crispy, flavorful crust.

The name “Six Hundred” nods to the oven’s scorching temperature, and with world pizza champion Brittany Saxton leading the kitchen, you’re in for a treat. It’s a must-visit for foodies exploring Columbus, Ohio’s nearby gems, and one of the fun things to do in Bellefontaine.

Brewfontaine: Ohio’s Premier Beer Bar

Brewfontaine was crowned “Ohio’s #1 Beer Bar,” and it lives up to the title. (We hear that a lot from people who stop in. Lots of "thumbs ups" and smiles.)

With a stellar lineup of craft beers, many from local Ohio breweries, it’s a must-experience spot for beer lovers.

Pair your pint with a juicy Smashburger, Reuben, or smoky BLT. Brewfontaine’s laid-back atmosphere is perfect for families, with kid-friendly options like fries and chicken tenders. Their “Paint Your Partner” event, held near Valentine’s Day, adds a creative twist, adding art and socializing for a unique experience.

(Keep that in mind when February rolls back around!)

The Syndicate: Elevated Dining

We may be a small town, but we offer it all. For a more upscale experience, The Syndicate fits the bill. Think perfectly cooked steaks, fresh seafood, and live music that rivals any downtown Columbus venue.

Its old-school charm, with historic touches like local newspapers and glass windows, mixes perfectly with modern elegance. Dive into mimosa flights, chicken and waffles, or butternut squash ravioli for a meal that wows you on your day trip. It’s cozy, yet sophisticated.

A perfect spot for a family dinner or date night.

Flying Pepper Cantina: Mexican Vibes

Craving something lively? Boy, can we give you that!

Flying Pepper Cantina serves up tacos, fajitas, and margaritas in a colorful setting. Founded by Humberto Nieto, who brings traditional recipes from Michoacán, Mexico, this spot uses fresh, local ingredients for authentic flavors. Its family-friendly atmosphere makes it a hit for all ages, perfect for a fun lunch or dinner during one of the best day trips from Columbus, Ohio.

Sweet Treats at Candy Craze

Got a sweet tooth? Candy Craze is a family-friendly candy store packed with nostalgic treats like Pop Rocks and a Pucker Power Station for custom mixes.

Owner Tanisha McCreary stocks local goodies and sugar-free options, ensuring everyone leaves happy. It’s a great stop to keep kids entertained after a day of exploring, adding to Bellefontaine’s reputation as a top destination for the best day trips.

Shopping in Bellefontaine’s Charming Downtown

Bellefontaine’s downtown shops are a major highlight. You’ll find boutique stores with unique finds, from handmade jewelry to vintage treasures, making Bellefontaine a standout for the best day trips. After shopping, unwind with a stroll at Indian Lake which offers walking trails and picnic areas.

Boutique Shops for Unique Finds

Browse Bellefontaine’s eclectic stores for one-of-a-kind items. Queen of Hearts Fine Chocolates & Gifts offers gourmet chocolates and hand-painted pottery, while Just U’Neeq specializes in trendy farmhouse décor.

Mystic Mama Rock + Jewelry showcases stunning semi-precious stones and global treasures, and The Naked Goat Bath & Body provides luxurious natural soaps and lotions. These shops are perfect for finding gifts or treating yourself during your visit.

Antiques and Vintage Treasures

Antique lovers will adore The Nest 1896, a cozy spot with over 55 vendors offering vintage clothes, jewelry, and crafts in a historic building. The Olde Mint Antiques is a three-story treasure trove of books, oddities, and quirky finds at fair prices. These spots are ideal for uncovering unique pieces you won’t find online, adding to the charm of Bellefontaine’s shopping scene.

Historical Gems in Bellefontaine

While historic sites like the Ohio State Reformatory or the Erie Canal are fascinating, Bellefontaine’s historical attractions are just as compelling and far more accessible. These landmarks add a touch of culture and art to your day trip without requiring a golf cart to navigate sprawling grounds.

America’s First Concrete Street

Bellefontaine is home to America’s First Concrete Street, a quirky piece of history perfect for a quick photo. It’s a fun contrast to the natural beauty of Ash Cave or Brandywine Falls, offering a unique story to share with friends. This small but significant landmark highlights Ohio’s role in pioneering infrastructure.

Logan County History Center

For a deeper look at the area’s past, head to the Logan County History Center. Housed in a beautifully preserved mansion just a few blocks from downtown, the museum showcases everything from Native American artifacts and military memorabilia to a recreated 1890s street scene and vintage vehicles. It’s a fascinating stop for history buffs and casual visitors alike, offering insight into Logan County’s rich heritage in a setting that’s both informative and easy to explore. Don’t miss the Transportation Museum next door—it’s a hit with families and car lovers.

The Holland Theatre: A Cultural Hub

For a dose of entertainment, visit The Holland Theatre, a historic venue from 1931 known for its Dutch-themed architecture. Its interior, resembling a quaint Dutch village, hosts Broadway-style shows, classic movies, and live performances. The theatre’s unique design and cultural offerings make it a standout stop for art lovers and a perfect way to relax during your trip.

Outdoor and Adventure Activities

Bellefontaine isn’t just about food and shopping—it’s also a gateway to outdoor adventures that complement its small-town charm. Whether you’re visiting in winter or spring, there’s something for everyone.

Ohio Caverns: Natural Wonders

Just outside Bellefontaine, Ohio Caverns is the state’s largest cave system, with over two miles of underground passageways. This natural wonder offers guided tours that reveal stunning formations and teach about the area’s geology. It’s a great addition to the best day trips, especially for families looking to explore beyond downtown.

Mad River Mountain: Winter Thrills

In winter, Mad River Mountain, a short drive from Bellefontaine, offers skiing and snowboarding. It’s a thrilling add-on for adventure seekers, but Bellefontaine’s year-round appeal keeps it the star of the best day trips. Even in spring, the area’s scenic beauty makes it worth a visit.

Indian Lake: Waterfront Views and Outdoor Fun

If you have extra time in your day, make the short drive north to Indian Lake—one of Ohio’s largest inland lakes and a favorite local getaway. With beaches, boat rentals, and walking trails, it’s a great spot to unwind and enjoy the water. Families can relax at Old Field Beach, grab a bite at lakeside restaurants, or watch the sunset from the shore. It’s a peaceful, scenic add-on to your Bellefontaine day trip, offering outdoor fun that feels like a mini vacation.

Marmon Valley Farm: Farm Fun for All Ages

Just a short drive from downtown Bellefontaine, Marmon Valley Farm offers a hands-on, family-friendly experience that blends outdoor fun with rural charm. Known for its horseback trail rides, petting zoo, and seasonal events, the farm is perfect for kids and adults looking to connect with nature. Whether you're feeding goats, riding ponies, or exploring the scenic grounds, it’s a refreshing break from the bustle of everyday life and a great way to round out your day trip.

Family-Friendly Fun in Bellefontaine

Bellefontaine is a top choice for the whole family. Tired of the Columbus Zoo or Ohio Bird Sanctuary? Bellefontaine offers a balanced mix of dining, shopping, and low-key attractions that keep everyone happy, making it ideal for the best day trips.

Kid-Friendly Activities

Kids will love the vibrant atmosphere at Flying Pepper Cantina and the chance to explore Bellefontaine’s parks. The Fun Company, a toy store with games, puzzles, and weekly events, is a great reward for kids after a day at Mad River Mountain.

Parents will appreciate Bellefontaine’s small-town ease. The town’s walkable layout and friendly locals make it a hassle-free destination, unlike the crowds at amusement parks. Whether you’re browsing shops or dining, Bellefontaine feels welcoming and manageable.

For families with older kids or thrill-seekers, Decode Zone offers affordable escape rooms like Spooky Forest or Chess Board. These mind-bending challenges are a fun way to keep the excitement going after hitting the slopes or exploring downtown. It’s a unique activity that adds to Bellefontaine’s diverse offerings.

Crafting Your Perfect Bellefontaine Itinerary

To make the most of your day trip, here’s a plan that balances food, shops, and sights, all while keeping Bellefontaine the star of the show:

Morning: Hit the Road

Take the scenic hour-long drive to Bellefontaine. It's a nice way to escape the daily routines.

Lunch: Brewfontaine’s Craft Beer Haven

Arrive in Bellefontaine and head to Brewfontaine for lunch. Sample a flight of craft beers and enjoy a hearty burger.

Afternoon: Shops and History

After lunch, explore Bellefontaine’s downtown shops for unique finds. Then, take a quick walk to America’s First Concrete Street and the Logan County Courthouse for a dose of history.

Evening: Dinner with Flair

End your day at The Syndicate for a steak dinner with live music, or opt for tacos at Flying Pepper Cantina. Don't forget the many dessert options!

Why Bellefontaine Feels Authentic

Unlike tourist-heavy spots like amusement parks, Bellefontaine feels genuine. The local restaurants and shops aren’t trying to be anything they’re not—they’re rooted in community pride, making your visit feel personal and meaningful. This authenticity is what makes Bellefontaine a top choice for the best day trips.

Bellefontaine vs. Other Central Ohio Destinations

Central Ohio has no shortage of day trip options, from Hocking Hills and Kingwood Center Gardens to West Liberty or Pyramid Hill Sculpture Park, but Bellefontaine’s unique charm sets it apart. You'll leave feeling fulfilled, happily surprised, and relaxed. Not exhausted.

You'll also go home knowing you experienced culinary delights and unique shopping without breaking the bank. No sticker shock here.

Bellefontaine delivers a perfect mix of food, shops, and history in one walkable town. Ohio’s role as a cradle of community-driven destinations shines through here.

Why Foodies Can’t Resist Bellefontaine

Foodies will find plenty to love in Bellefontaine’s restaurants. The variety—craft beer, upscale steaks, and Mexican flair—sets it apart. Each meal is an experience that makes your trip unforgettable, with local ingredients and passionate chefs at the helm.

Bellefontaine’s Community Charm

The warmth of Bellefontaine’s community is what makes it special. From shop owners to restaurant staff, everyone welcomes you like a friend. This sense of connection is harder to find in busier destinations like Yellow Springs or Richland County, making Bellefontaine a true gem for visitors.

Art and Culture in Bellefontaine

Bellefontaine’s cultural scene is vibrant, with opportunities to engage with art at every turn. From the “Paint Your Partner” event at Brewfontaine to the hand-painted pottery at Queen of Hearts, the town celebrates creativity. The Holland Theatre’s performances and unique Dutch architecture further elevate the art scene, offering a cultural depth that rivals larger cities.

Exploring Logan County’s Broader Appeal

Bellefontaine serves as the heart of Logan County, a region rich with attractions that complement the town’s offerings. Whether you’re visiting Ohio Caverns or enjoying a meal at The Syndicate, Logan County’s diversity makes it a hub for the best day trips. The area’s mix of natural beauty, history, and community spirit ensures there’s always something new to discover.

Tips for a Seamless Day Trip

To ensure your Bellefontaine adventure goes smoothly, keep these tips in mind:

Pack Smart

Bring a water bottle, comfy shoes, and a light jacket. If you were heading to Hocking Hills for a picnic, you’d need more gear, but Bellefontaine’s compact nature keeps things simple.

Check Hours

Some shops and restaurants have specific hours, so visit experiencelogancounty.com for details. This is easier than planning around the seasonal schedules of Mad River Mountain.

Share Your Experience

Snap photos of your food, shops, and sights, and share them with #CentralOhioDayTrips. It’s a fun way to inspire others to visit Bellefontaine, unlike the crowded posts from the Columbus Zoo.

Final Thoughts: Make Bellefontaine Your Next Trip

While Central Ohio offers countless day trip options, from the Erie Canal to Hocking Hills, Bellefontaine is the ultimate destination for food, shops, and charm. It’s a great place for the whole family to enjoy a day of adventure, without the crowds of amusement parks or the sprawl of Cuyahoga Valley National Park.

Pack the car, plan your visit, and dive into Bellefontaine’s unique offerings. Check out experiencelogancounty.com for menus, maps, and more, and share your journey with #CentralOhioDayTrips. Your perfect day trip awaits in Bellefontaine—don’t miss it!

item.title: Discover Unique Shopping Near Columbus Ohio: Bellefontaine
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Bored with the same old shopping in the capital city? Not loving what you're finding in Downtown Columbus? Seen everything at Easton Town Center? Polaris Fashion Place not doing it for you? We get it. You're looking for something new.

Located an hour northwest of Columbus, Bellefontaine is a charming small town that offers wonderful shops and experiences that redefine boutique shopping. It's a perfect day trip for a fun Saturday.

Or any day of the week, for that matter.

Bellefontaine’s walkable downtown is the perfect place for unique shops. Oh, and it's an absolute haven for antique lovers.

Actually, it's a haven for a lot of people.

With vibrant specialty shops, historic allure, and a good selection of one-of-a-kind gifts, it’s a must-visit for Central Ohio shoppers looking for retail offerings that will grab their attention and curiosity. The town’s art-filled streets and welcoming atmosphere make it an absolute must-visit for those who value local businesses over national brands.

Did we mention that the helpful staff in the shops further enhance the shopping experience? You'll see how they provide guidance and a friendly ambiance.

Introduction to Bellefontaine

Bellefontaine offers boutique shopping, rich history, and natural beauty, making it a must-stop for anyone looking to explore the best of Central Ohio. The local and family-owned businesses in Bellefontaine not only offer unique products and services, but they also play a vital role in the community's culture and heritage.

Whether you’re wandering through the walkable downtown area, filled with locally owned shops, or enjoying all the nearby popular destinations, Bellefontaine promises a shopping experience that’s both intimate and exciting.

From one-of-a-kind crafts to unique gifts, Bellefontaine’s downtown streets offer a refreshing alternative to the homogenized national brands found in Columbus. Bellefontaine is also a fun stop for visitors, offering an enjoyable shopping experience with its friendly atmosphere and diverse inventory.

Don't get us wrong; Columbus is great and has so much to offer. But once you step into Bellefontaine, you'll ask yourself one question:

"Why didn't we find this sooner?"

The Revitalization of Downtown Bellefontaine

Thanks to revitalization efforts, Bellefontaine’s downtown has transformed 58 buildings, including the historic opera block now bustling with 13 specialty shops. This revival, supported by the local community, makes Bellefontaine a hub for local businesses’ retail offerings, surpassing Columbus’s chain-dominated scene.

The streets of downtown, alive with art and community spirit, showcase a good selection of unique gifts that draw shoppers away from the same old national brands. The revitalized buildings house one-of-a-kind boutiques, ensuring every street corner offers something special. Visitors can also create personalized experiences in these unique shops.

Looking for new events to check out? Visit the “Downtown Days Bellefontaine” Facebook page (insert link).

Historic Charm Meets Modern Shopping

Bellefontaine is perched at 1,600 feet on Campbell Hill, Ohio’s highest point. The town is home to McKinley Street, America’s shortest at just 16 feet, adorned with a distinctive mural that’s a must for photos, and the nation’s first concrete street, paved in 1891 by George Bartholomew. Adding to its allure, the stately Logan County Courthouse and these unique attractions provide a picturesque backdrop for shopping and exploration, outshining even German Village’s cobblestone streets.

Why Choose Locally Owned Shops

Bellefontaine’s locally owned shops offer a personal touch you won’t find in big-box stores. From curated clothing to fine antiques, these specialty shops cater to every taste, ensuring each purchase you make feels meaningful and unique.

(After all, isn’t that what you want? These are the goodies you'll take home, each one giving you that cozy, happy vibe when you see them.)

Many customers have shared stories of special items they purchased, like a handmade hat that sparked a new passion or a vintage cash register that helped restore a general store. The streets of Bellefontaine are lined with shops that prioritize art and craftsmanship, offering a good selection of unique gifts that make every visit memorable.

And remember: Choosing local over national brands supports the community and ensures a one-of-a-kind shopping experience.

The Nest 1896: A Shopper’s Paradise

The Nest 1896 is a charming, farmhouse-inspired boutique nestled in a beautifully renovated historic building from 1896.

Spanning three floors, the shop showcases an eclectic mix of vintage treasures, handmade goods, locally crafted items, architectural salvage, and unique project pieces. It's a great place for families, with unique items that can spark joy for any kid.

With over 55 vendors, The Nest offers something for everyone—primitive antiques, vintage vinyl records, handmade jewelry, boutique women’s clothing, locally made bath and body products, and artisanal food items.

Fine Antiques at The Olde Mint

The Olde Mint Antiques is a treasure trove for collectors and nostalgia enthusiasts alike.

With over 30 dealers spread across three sprawling floors, this antique mall boasts an impressive selection of vintage finds, from rare books to unique collectibles, all at remarkably reasonable prices.

Wandering through its aisles is a delightful adventure, where every corner holds the promise of uncovering your next cherished gem. Whether you’re a seasoned collector or simply love the thrill of the hunt, the enjoyable wait while exploring the aisles adds to the thrill of finding unique items.

Indulge at Queen of Hearts

At Queen of Hearts Fine Chocolates & Gifts, delight in gourmet chocolates and explore a charming gift shop filled with unique treasures like hand-painted pottery and MacKenzie-Childs designs. You'll look at (and enjoy the aroma of) luxurious handcrafted sweets and curated gifts, perfect for any occasion. What do they have? Here's a peek: DeBrand’s Fine Chocolates, Dietsch Brothers, MacKenzie-Childs, C. Krueger Cookies, and more!

Sustainable Shopping at The Naked Goat

The Naked Goat Bath & Body is all about helping you feel good in your own skin with self-care that’s clean, kind, and oh-so-nourishing.

How? They pour their heart into creating products packed with gentle, skin-loving ingredients, like the magic of goat milk that hydrates and calms like a warm hug. Every lotion, soap, and candle is made to turn your everyday routine into a chance to pause, pamper, and just breathe.

They’ve handpicked some amazing brands to share with you, like Beekman 1802, Farmhouse Fresh, and Voluspa Candles, and more. Eco-conscious shoppers will love the chance to purchase eco-friendly products (they’re all-natural), setting this shop apart from the typical shopping in Columbus.

Family Fun at The Fun Company

The Fun Company, a toy store with weekly events, is a hit with kids and parents. Its unique toys and engaging activities make it a prime spot to purchase gifts for your kids.

Warning: They'll want to come back, and they'll bring it up repeatedly. But isn't that kind of memory a wonderful thing?

The Fun Company offers a good selection of one-of-a-kind toys that spark joy. Parents can find unique gifts that make playtime special, setting this shop apart from the mass-produced offerings of national brands.

Sip and Stroll with DORA

The Designated Outdoor Refreshment Area (DORA) lets you sip craft brews from Brewfontaine—voted Ohio’s top beer bar for five years—while browsing specialty shops.

That's right: The top beer bar in the entire state is right here in Bellefontaine. What's all the buzz about? You'll have to step in and find out for yourself!

Visitors can also enjoy a variety of restaurants, enhancing their overall experience as they shop and explore. This laid-back vibe makes Bellefontaine a great alternative to the Short North’s crowded streets. Strolling down Main Street with a drink in hand, you’ll discover art galleries and unique shops you'll long remember.

We say that because that's what we keep hearing from others; they're making memories here. And if you want to make memories too, allow us to make a few more tiny suggestions.

Kick Off Your Day at Sweet Aromas

Begin your shopping adventure at Sweet Aromas Coffee, a great place where cozy lattes and cappuccinos set the tone. Its warm ambiance beats the generic coffee stops at Easton Town Center.

Don't believe us? Compare the two for yourself.

Morning Boutique Exploration

Spend your morning wandering downtown’s specialty shops. Hit The Nest 1896 for trendy clothing, and The Olde Mint to purchase fine antiques that will give your home the vibe you've been wanting. These shops offer a great selection of items, ensuring you find something unique and special.

Lunch Break at Flying Pepper Cantina

For lunch, Flying Pepper Cantina offers diverse dining options with authentic Mexican dishes made from fresh, local ingredients. It’s a tastier, more affordable option than dining in the Short North Arts District.

Pizza Perfection at Six Hundred Downtown

Alternatively, Six Hundred Downtown, led by six-time world pizza champion Brittany Saxton, is a great spot offering hand-spun pizzas baked at 600 degrees.

Did you catch that? We said, "World Pizza Champion." One bite, and you'll understand why. The bold flavors outshine German Village’s dining scene.

(That's right, we've got the best beer bar in Ohio and the woman who has earned the title of "world champion" when it comes to pizza!)

The Syndicate: Where History Meets Big City Dining

Once Jackson's News Stand (1946-1993), The Syndicate has both nostalgic charm and modern elegance at the same time. You'll immediately notice a custom bar with salvaged glass and quartz countertops, surrounded by vintage Ohio newspapers that tell Bellefontaine’s story.

Savor fresh, gourmet food crafted by talented chefs, from brunch to dinner, paired with signature cocktails. Whether it’s a casual meal, happy hour, or a catered event, The Syndicate is Ohio’s go-to for unforgettable food and vibes.

Challenge Your Mind at Decode Zone's Escape Rooms

Ready for a thrilling adventure? Head to Decode Zone for fun, affordable escape rooms that keep the excitement flowing! Check out the following options:

  • Spooky Forest: Race against time to break a mysterious curse.
  • Chess Board: Outsmart a mind-bending twist on classic games.
  • Spice Cookies: Solve the mystery of the missing treat.
  • Mechanic Wrench: Tackle a gear-filled, mechanic-themed challenge.

With immersive experiences for all, there’s something for everyone to enjoy.

Capture Memories at Historic Sites

Get photos at McKinley Street’s mural for your social media feed. The street’s art-filled charm and historic allure make it a perfect spot for capturing memories and enviable Instagram or TikTok posts.

Sweet Endings at Candy Craze

Cap your day at Candy Craze, where nostalgic treats and custom candy mixes await. (We're itching for a treat just writing this!) It’s the perfect place to purchase a sweet memento of your Bellefontaine adventure, with each treat purchased adding a special touch to your experience.

Cultural Gem: Holland Theatre

Check the Holland Theatre’s schedule for shows like a magic performance or an Eagles tribute. Its Dutch-themed design, complete with starry projections, outshines Short North’s venues. Step in and you'll say five words:

"Oh, I get it now."

Pineapple Palooza!

The Pineapple Palooza, a vibrant summer street festival, honors the quirky pineapple atop the courthouse fountain in downtown Bellefontaine. According to local lore, when the fountain was installed in the 1980s, a bell was intended to crown it. Instead, Bellefontaine’s bell was sent to Hawaii, and a pineapple took its place. This charming tale spread through the community, inspiring the pineapple to become a central feature of the downtown Bellefontaine logo.

Bellefontaine vs. Columbus Retail

Unlike Columbus’s retail parks, Bellefontaine’s intimate, locally owned shops offer unmatched personal service. Shop owners’ expertise in clothing and fine antiques creates a refreshing contrast to chain stores that you've seen again and again.

Fun Events and Nearby Adventures

Events like Pineapple Palooza, inspired by the courthouse fountain’s quirky pineapple topper, bring music, food trucks, and pineapple-themed fun. And remember, nearby Ohio Caverns and Marmon Valley Farm for horseback riding make Bellefontaine an ideal base.

Bellefontaine’s Unique Appeal

Bellefontaine’s boutique scene offers a refreshing escape from Columbus’s crowded malls, featuring hidden gems that provide unique and delightful experiences. While Easton Town Center has designer brands, Bellefontaine’s specialty shops deliver unique clothing and gifts with a personal touch. The town’s art-filled streets and welcoming atmosphere make it a haven for shoppers seeking one-of-a-kind finds.

Outshining German Village

German Village’s historic charm is lovely, but Bellefontaine’s walkable downtown and historic landmarks like the courthouse make it a great place to visit. Local businesses’ dedication ensures every purchase feels special.

A Day Trip Worth Taking

Visiting Bellefontaine’s downtown offers an unforgettable experience, whether you’re after clothing, antiques, or local crafts. Its specialty shops, historic charm, and vibrant events make it a cut above Columbus’s retail districts. The art-filled streets and one-of-a-kind gifts ensure a day well spent.

Evening Entertainment Options

After shopping, catch a show at Holland Theatre or sip cocktails at The Syndicate. Art galleries in the area also provide a cultural touch, blending seamlessly with specialty shops and dining options for a full-day experience. Bellefontaine’s evening scene, set against art-filled streets, offers more charm than Short North’s overcrowded bars.

Why Bellefontaine Is a Must-See

Bellefontaine’s blend of historic charm, specialty shops, and local dining creates a shopping experience that’s both intimate and exciting, filled with hidden gems waiting to be discovered. It’s the perfect place to escape Columbus’s retail monotony. The streets, alive with art and one-of-a-kind gifts, make every visit unforgettable.

Shopping for Every Style

From The Nest 1896’s trendy clothing to The Olde Mint’s vintage finds, Bellefontaine offers a great selection that caters to every shopper. Its diverse retail offerings ensure there’s something for everyone to purchase. The art-filled streets and unique gifts make Bellefontaine a shopper’s paradise.

A Community-Centered Experience

Local businesses’ passion for community shines in every shop. Whether you’re buying clothing at The Nest 1896 or toys at The Fun Company you’re supporting Bellefontaine’s vibrant economy. The streets, adorned with art, foster a sense of connection while offering unique gifts.

Beyond Shopping: Outdoor Fun

Nearby locations like Ohio Caverns or Marmon Valley Farm add adventure to your trip. Bellefontaine’s mix of shopping and outdoor activities makes it a better day trip than Columbus’s urban sprawl. The streets, filled with art and unique gifts, serve as a gateway to unforgettable experiences.

Plan Your Visit Today

Ready to ditch the mall and start visiting Bellefontaine’s boutique scene? Visit downtownbellefontaine.com for a shop directory and map. Share your favorite finds with #ShopBellefontaine and tell us which specialty shop won your heart! The art-filled streets and one-of-a-kind gifts await your discovery.

item.title: Pineapple Palooza Returns to Downtown Bellefontaine June 7
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Get ready for our biggest event of the year!

Downtown Bellefontaine is about to transform into a tropical paradise for a full day of summer fun, community connection, and high-energy entertainment. Pineapple Palooza returns on Saturday, June 7 from 2 PM to Midnight, and this year’s event is shaping up to be bigger and better than ever.

From bounce houses and obstacle courses to ziplining down Main Street, Pineapple Palooza is packed with activities for all ages. It’s more than a street festival—it’s a full-blown summer celebration that brings together families, local businesses, visitors, and the entire community for one unforgettable day.

GET TICKETS!

A Full Day of Family Fun
The fun starts at 2 PM with a wide range of attractions for kids and adults alike. You can:

  • Ride a zipline through downtown
  • Explore bounce houses and obstacle courses
  • Meet animals in the exotic petting zoo
  • Enjoy horseback riding
  • Try your luck at the dunk tank
  • Participate in line dancing, games, and contests
  • Shop at vendor booths and the new Lil’ Pineapple Pop-Up Market

Attractions tickets are available for just $15, which includes access to all bounce houses, obstacle courses, and horseback rides. Want to fly over Main Street? Add the zipline for just $15 more—or purchase a zipline-only ticket if you're just in it for the thrill.

GET TICKETS!

Live Music & Headline Concert
The energy ramps up in the evening with live music, DJ sets, and performances throughout the night, including John Whitehead, Just Darcy, and Pete Moss. At 9 PM, don’t miss our free headline concert featuring Southern Summer, a country rock band known for their high-energy shows. Bring your lawn chairs and stay late—the party goes until 11:30 PM.

Little Miss & Mister Pineapple Contest
One of the most beloved parts of the day is the Little Miss & Mister Pineapple Contest, happening at 3 PM across from the courthouse. Kids will answer fun, personality-based questions in a friendly showcase with prizes for all participants. It's free to enter, and pre-registration is encouraged.

Young Entrepreneurs Wanted
This year introduces the Lil’ Pineapple Pop-Up Market, a special vendor area just for kidpreneurs. Young makers, bakers, and creators will set up shop and sell their goods. It’s a fun way to support the next generation of small business owners.

GET TICKETS!

Join Us June 7
Pineapple Palooza is presented by Four Acre Clothing Co. and their family of businesses, including Hi-Point Graphics, Hi Point Business Solutions, and All-Around Awards. It’s a day packed with excitement, and we’d love for you to be a part of it.

See you in Downtown Bellefontaine on Saturday, June 7!

item.title: Cracked Pot Studio Partners with Triple Moon Design Co. to Bring Paint-Your-Own Pottery to Downtown Bellefontaine
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
June 9, 2025 - A new creative collaboration is taking shape in Downtown Bellefontaine. Cracked Pot Studio and Triple Moon Design Co. are teaming up to bring a permanent paint-your-own pottery experience to the heart of downtown.

Thelma Matthews, founder of Cracked Pot Studio, has been a fixture in Bellefontaine’s art scene for decades. Known for her expertise in pottery and her ability to draw artists from around the country to her studio, Thelma has built a space that inspires beginners and professionals alike. Now, she’s expanding her vision by moving her walk-in pottery painting studio into Triple Moon Design Co. at 117 W. Columbus Ave.

“Partnering with Jessica and Triple Moon is a natural next step for Cracked Pot,” said Thelma. “It allows us to reach more people, right in the middle of the energy and foot traffic of downtown. I’ve always believed art should be accessible and fun, and this partnership gives us a new way to welcome people in.”

Triple Moon Design Co. has become known as a creative retail destination, offering a curated selection of gifts, lifestyle goods, and experiences - including its popular candle lab. The new paint-your-own pottery studio will operate much the same way: walk in off the street, choose a pottery piece, paint at your own pace, and return later to pick up your kiln-fired creation.

“This has been a dream in the making,” said Jessica Epley, owner of Triple Moon Design Co. “Thelma’s work and passion for pottery are so inspiring, and we’re honored to help carry that forward. We’re always looking for ways to add more creativity and community connection to our space, and this is the perfect fit.”

The new walk-in pottery experience officially opened last weekend. Thelma will frequently be at Triple Moon to help welcome guests to the space.

Cracked Pot Studio will continue its operations at Union Station in Bellefontaine, where Thelma hosts advanced pottery workshops, national artists, and sells locally made ceramic goods. The downtown location will focus on making pottery accessible to everyone, from kids to curious first-timers. For more information, visit www.triplemoonshop.com or follow @triplemoondesignco on Facebook.

item.title: New Event Venue in Bellefontaine, Ohio!
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Bellefontaine, Ohio, is a city where history and progress intertwine, and a recent transformation beautifully illustrates this. A historic church, once a place of worship, is being reborn to host memorable experiences for all who enter ...

... Perhaps even you!

The Maxwell in Bellefontaine, Ohio Has Everyone Ready

The Grace A.M.E. Church in downtown Bellefontaine, located at 233 South Main Street, is being converted into The Maxwell, a stunning new wedding and events venue. This venture is a partnership between Small Nation and Brittany Saxton, owner of Bellefontaine's popular restaurant, Six Hundred Downtown.

The name The Maxwell pays homage to Rev. W.T. Maxwell, the pastor who oversaw the church's original construction in 1890. Designed to be a premier event space, The Maxwell will be capable of hosting a wide array of gatherings, from elegant weddings to corporate events and social celebrations.

And guess what? It’s within walking distance of lodging. Check out the Cobblestone Hotel & Suites, which offers modern amenities and is just a short stroll away. Another convenient choice is the Flats at Rainbow Row, providing quality short-term rentals right in the downtown near The Maxwell!

But before we look ahead, let's wind back the clocks.

History of the Church

The Grace A.M.E. Church was founded way back in 1865, right after the Civil War ended and slavery was abolished. This was obviously a very important time for African Americans, and the A.M.E. church denomination itself played a huge role in fighting slavery and even helping people escape through the Underground Railroad.

Anyone standing in the building is surrounded by history.

Impactful, emotional, life-changing history.

Folks from the congregation, including women who raised money by doing things like laundry, and other community members all chipped in with their time and effort to build it.

Preserving Bellefontaine’s Heritage on Main St.

Even though this church is undergoing a transformation, the project respects the building's past. Jason Duff, Founder and CEO of Small Nation, has a clear vision for the new venue. He wants to honor what came before and embrace the future. How so?

The Maxwell will combine the church’s historic look with modern amenities.

Planned work includes a new patio, along with a seasonal tent conveniently located behind the building. This tent will be ideal for accommodating larger events in Bellefontaine during the warmer months. The space is being meticulously designed to create a stunning backdrop for unforgettable experiences, mixing historical charm with contemporary comforts.

Cally Lange of Revival Design Collective will be the lead architect for The Maxwell, and this is a good choice because Lange's firm is known for historic preservation.

On the construction side, Level MB Construction is responsible for the meticulous build, turning all those designs into reality. Small Nation served as the development partner, guiding the project from concept to completion.

And finally, Quest Federal Credit Union provided the essential financing that made the entire endeavor possible.

Looking for construction updates?

Visit The Maxwell Events Facebook page for the very latest, including photos!

Meeting the Demand in Downtown Bellefontaine

So, why is this happening? Simple. Downtown Bellefontaine needs more event spaces!

The Maxwell will meet that need and enhance every occasion with catering partners. The city is growing, and with that growth comes the need for quality services. The Maxwell will be a beautiful and versatile space for weddings, celebrations, and events.

And even though this will be a major draw, there's a lot more to Bellefontaine. Like we said, the town is growing ... and for good reason.

The Town's Other Attractions: Holland Theatre and More!

For example, the Holland Theatre offers great performances for those tired of the same movies and offerings.

Here's another question for you: Are you looking for some new content that will be popular on your social media channels? The Logan County Transportation Museum has a lot of fascinating exhibits. Take a step back in time, ask your parents and grandparents what memories these bring back, and you may just learn more about your own family too!

Shopping and Services: Nail Bar on Columbus Ave. and More!

Do you love shopping?

Downtown Bellefontaine has a lot to offer in that category, and there are some items here you won't find around Columbus, Lima, Dayton, or the other large surrounding cities. Come for a visit, and you'll see for yourself. People come to Bellefontaine, Ohio for something different ... both in terms of the products and the experience itself.

You can lose yourself for hours in places like The Nest 1896, a multi-floor boutique housed in a historic building. Take in farmhouse-inspired decor, vintage treasures, handmade goods, and items from over 55 vendors.

For antique lovers, The Olde Mint Antiques is a must-visit, offering an impressive selection of vintage finds across three sprawling floors.

If you're looking for delightful sweet treats and unique gifts, Queen of Hearts Fine Chocolates & Gifts is the perfect spot. And for those seeking fun and engaging items for children, The Fun Company is packed with one-of-a-kind toys, games, and books.

Another big draw: Relax Nail Bar and Salon, commonly referred to as "The Nail Bar." Located at 130 W Columbus Ave, this establishment offers a comprehensive range of services including manicures, pedicures, hair treatments, and waxing, all within a comfortable and welcoming environment. They aim to provide a luxurious experience for clients seeking various beauty services.

We should also mention Skin Sanctuary Boutique Spa, located in downtown Bellefontaine, Ohio. This is a place where you can go to get your skin looking and feeling its best. They offer a variety of services, from facials to treatments that help with things like wrinkles, acne, or sun damage.

They also do cosmetic tattooing, like microblading for your eyebrows or lip blush, to enhance your natural features. (Ever heard of cosmetic tattooing? No? Google it!)

Plus, you can get services like waxing, lash lifts, and lash tints. People often rave about how the staff really listens to their concerns and helps them achieve their skin goals. You can grab a coffee at the local cafe just around the corner from Columbus Ave.

Food and Family Fun

The food scene in Bellefontaine, Ohio, is booming. People come here to eat well, and once you experience it for yourself, you'll understand why.

Among the notable options are The Syndicate, offering a contemporary American dining experience in a setting that offers both history with modern elegance, and The Morning Riot, a unique brunch spot with an "alien disco" theme and a focus on fresh, locally sourced ingredients.

Alien disco? You read that right.

These establishments, alongside popular spots like Six Hundred Downtown known for its award-winning pizzas, and The Flying Pepper Cantina with its authentic Mexican cuisine and uber-popular cocktails, among many more, are part of why so many people take daytrips here.

There is a wide spread of restaurants to choose from, from casual to fine dining. The town also has family-friendly attractions. The Putt and Play Golf Center is a cool place for the whole family to spend some time. A day of putt-putt can be a lot of fun.

In Downtown Bellefontaine, families can unleash their creativity at The Fun Company, a toy store with interactive play areas and a wide selection of games. For a puzzling challenge, Decode Zone Escape Room provides immersive, family-friendly escape room experiences.

Places to Stay

Visitors to Bellefontaine, Ohio have more and more options for lodging. Whether you’re in town for a wedding at The Maxwell or just visiting, you can find a place to stay.

The Cobblestone Hotel & Suites - Bellefontaine is a newer addition and is often highlighted for its amenities like an indoor pool, fitness center, complimentary breakfast, and its proximity to downtown attractions. .

Bellefontaine’s Future

The future is bright for Bellefontaine, Ohio. With The Maxwell, the city is attracting new visitors and residents. Downtown is becoming a hot spot, and it's easy to see why. After all, the services are diverse, the shopping is great, and the food is downright exquisite.

And the town's new event center will push things forward even more.

We have to admit ... this is an exciting time!

Plan Your Event Now

Let's face it, demand for this venue is going to be HUGE. And if you want to claim your place for your next event, visit TheMaxwellEvents.com!

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: The historic Grace A.M.E. Church in Bellefontaine, built in the late 1800s, is being transformed into a new wedding and events venue called the Maxwell.

item.title: Tavien St Clair: Ohio State's Next Star Hails from Bellefontaine
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Tavien St. Clair, who hails from right here in Bellefontaine, is a name that's going to be at the center of countless conversations this fall. This freshman quarterback had options, but he chose the Buckeyes ... and all of Central Ohio is excited. He's another weapon in the arsenal as Ohio State pushes for yet another national championship. (Can they really do it again?) With St. Clair on the field, they've got a killer chance.

Tavien St. Clair: More Than Just a Five-Star Recruit

The “five-star” label doesn’t fully capture Tavien St. Clair. He’s not just a name talent; he’s a dedicated player with a very prolific background.

Check it out: His high school career saw him accumulate over 10,000 passing yards and 100 touchdown passes. His senior season in 2024 was strong, with 2,536 yards and 29 touchdowns, but his junior year in 2023 stood out, as he completed an impressive 70.6% of his passes for 3,083 yards and 37 touchdowns, adding 352 rushing yards and four scores. Buckeye fans are waiting to see if this all translates to how he performs at Ohio Stadium and beyond.

These consistent high-level performances earned him numerous accolades, including being a finalist for the 2024 Ohio Mr. Football, a three-time Central Buckeye Conference Offensive Player of the Year, and a two-time First-Team All-State quarterback, as well as the MVP of the Rivals Five Star Camp in June 2024.

Beyond the impressive stats, St. Clair possesses the ideal physical attributes and arm talent for a collegiate quarterback. Standing at 6-foot-4 and 225 pounds, he has the prototypical size, with a strong arm that generates impressive velocity with a tight spiral. And his functional mobility allows him to extend plays and gain yards with his legs when necessary.

His decision to commit to Ohio State over numerous other top programs speaks volumes about his potential and the belief in his abilities. He even ranked as the No. 1 prospect in the Rivals 250 at one point.

Why Ohio State University is the Right Fit

When deciding where to land, St. Clair had to think through a lot. Yes, football is important, but it was more than that. It was about finding a place where he could grow as a player and as a person. The academic rigor and the history of the Buckeyes program were big factors in his decision. He is clearly dedicated to succeeding both on and off the field. Off the field, he's studying business and sports management.

His parents, Marcus and Patricia St. Clair, were instrumental in helping him make this big decision. They looked at all the options, from his rights as a student-athlete to his long-term professional prospects.

Tavien St. Clair, Inc.

St. Clair is about to become his own brand. If he meets the hype, he could become the next Columbus legend. Expectations (naturally for Ohio State football) are astronomical. Fair or not, it's part of the gig in Columbus. And as a big figure now in college football, he's ready for all those eyes to be on him. As his reputation grows, a lot of people will be talking about his hometown of Bellefontaine.

Come to Bellefontaine Yourself!

A lot of people are learning about why St. Clair's hometown is so magnetic. It offers incredible food, like the championship pizzas at Six Hundred Downtown or the award-winning craft beers at Brewfontaine. (Did you know they have been voted the #1 Beer Bar in Ohio by CraftBeer.com? It's official.) You can explore unique shops, from the charming Queen of Hearts Fine Chocolates & Gifts to the treasure trove, The Nest 1896.

Plus, you can see history itself with America’s First Concrete Street and the stunning Logan County Courthouse...or even catch a show at the beautifully restored Holland Theatre.

If you love Ohio State football and want to see where one of its current stars hails from, check out Bellefontaine. You'll be glad you did.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Learn more about this Bellefontaine native, and how he's going to be the next big thing not only at Ohio State but all of college football.

wp:comment.wp:comment_author: Bellefontaine, Ohio and Football: Our First-Ever Tailgate Takeover is Almost Here - Downtown Bellefontaine
wp:comment.wp:comment_content: […] Find your spot downtown to cheer on Bellefontaine’s own Tavien St. Clair, playing for the Ohio State Buckeyes! […]

item.title: Movies Filmed in Ohio: Bellefontaine Serves as Film Location
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Ohio has a rich cinematic history, with cameras rolling on iconic films like A Christmas Story, The Shawshank Redemption, and Rain Man. Adding to this legacy is the recently filmed Union County, starring Will Poulter, known for his role in Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3, and Noah Centineo, who’s generating buzz for his upcoming part in the Street Fighter reboot.

Shot largely in Bellefontaine, Union County is a heartfelt story inspired by real-life recovery journeys from the local community. This film is putting Bellefontaine on the map, making it a must-visit for movie buffs.

Movies Filmed in Ohio: The Heart of Union County

Union County isn’t just another Hollywood product.

This one’s personal.

Director Adam Meeks shot it in Logan and Union Counties from April 21 to May 16, 2025. Will Poulter plays Cody Parsons, a man on a recovery journey inspired by real Logan County recovery stories, as he confronts the shadows of his past while seeking a new beginning. Locals aren’t just extras—they’re essentially playing themselves, sharing their lives with a raw honesty that you’ll feel in your heart when you watch it on the big screen.

This is the kind of film that gets inside your soul, not just another thoughtless reboot that is the typical Hollywood fare these days..

Meeks, who grew up in Ohio, drew inspiration from local recovery programs and personal stories shared during community meetings held before production began. These gatherings allowed residents to contribute ideas, ensuring the script reflected the town’s spirit.

Hollywood Comes to Town

When word spread that Hollywood was coming to Bellefontaine, many people nearly spilled their coffee. Will Poulter, from The Maze Runner and Midsommar, is filming here? No way!

The town beat out other Buckeye cities that are often used as filming locations, including Columbus, Cleveland, and Cincinnati.

The crew took over downtown Brewfontaine, along with some quiet backroads. Back in 2024, Poulter and Centineo were spotted at Brewfontaine scouting locations, sipping craft beers and chatting with locals.

The production generated excitement in Bellefontaine. Restaurants like Six Hundred Downtown saw an uptick in customers as crew members grabbed meals between takes, while shops like The Nest 1896 sold souvenirs to visitors.

The rest of Bellefontaine's businesses also saw increased activity during the shoot, as the production made use of various locations around town. For travelers, this Hollywood connection makes Bellefontaine a unique destination, where you can sip coffee in the same spots as movie stars.

Ohio’s Cinematic Legacy

Ohio’s been a Hollywood favorite for years, with cameras rolling across the state. A Christmas Story (1983), that holiday classic we’ve all seen at least 100,000 times, where Ralphie begs for a Red Ryder BB gun, was filmed in Cleveland’s Tremont neighborhood and downtown Cleveland (even though the story is set in a fictional town in Indiana).

Do you have a leg lamp as a holiday decoration sitting in the window of your house? So many people do! And we can all hear Santa Claus saying, "You'll shoot your eye out, kid!"

Cleveland’s Tremont neighborhood, with its historic architecture, served as the perfect backdrop for the film’s iconic scenes, making it synonymous with Ohio’s Christmas movie history.

The Shawshank Redemption, starring Tim Robbins and Morgan Freeman, turned the Ohio State Reformatory in Mansfield into a cinematic landmark. (The Ohio State Reformatory is located in Richland County.)

Big names like Tom Cruise and Dustin Hoffman in Rain Man, Harrison Ford in Air Force One, and Christopher Walken in The Deer Hunter have all acted out iconic roles within the Buckeye State, too.

Rain Man (1988) used Cincinnati locations like the Dixie Terminal Building and St. Anne Convent for key scenes with Cruise. Air Force One (1997) featured Ford in sequences shot at Cleveland’s Severance Hall, the Cuyahoga County Courthouse, and Mansfield’s Ohio State Reformatory.

The Deer Hunter (1978) included Walken in scenes shot at St. Theodosius Russian Orthodox Cathedral in Cleveland, known for its distinctive Russian architecture and copper onion domes, as well as Lemko Hall and Mingo Junction, with Ohio’s U.S. Steel Central Blast Furnace as a backdrop.

(It's a must-see for any Walken or Robert De Niro fans.)

Lakewood, a Cleveland suburb, was a location for American Splendor (2003), a biographical film about Harvey Pekar, starring Paul Giamatti, showcasing both Cleveland and Lakewood. Judas and the Black Messiah (2021) was shot in Cleveland, depicting the history of the Black Panther Party, a revolutionary organization active in the 1960s and 1970s advocating for African American rights and social justice in Chicago and other cities. For the production, Cleveland was transformed to resemble 1960s Chicago.

And did you know that Captain America: The Winter Soldier (2014) also used Cleveland as a location? Other Ohio classics include Major League (1989), shot at Cleveland’s Municipal Stadium, and Super 8 (2011), shot in Weirton, just across the Ohio River.

The Ohio Film Office attracts productions with tax incentives, contributing to hundreds of productions setting foot in Ohio since the 1970s.

Modern Blockbusters Shot in Ohio

And while Cleveland has been popular, Cincinnati has also played a starring role in recent years. The critically acclaimed film Carol (2015), featuring Cate Blanchett and Rooney Mara, was shot in locations such as Eden Park and the Over-the-Rhine neighborhood. The film’s lush visuals and period details were brought to life by Cincinnati’s timeless architecture and cityscape.

Other major productions have taken advantage of Ohio’s diverse scenery. Spider-Man 3 (2007) swung through Cleveland for some of its most memorable action sequences, while Unstoppable (2010) used various Ohio locations to capture the intensity of its runaway train drama. Even the scenic stretches of the Appalachian Trail have made appearances, showcasing the state’s natural beauty.

In fact, Ohio has seen many of the silver screen's most impactful stars and directors: Cate Blanchett, Barry Levinson, Robert De Niro, George Clooney, Anthony Russo, Morgan Freeman, Tim Robbins, Rooney Mara, and more.

Action and Drama: Ohio’s Genre Highlights

When it comes to versatility, Ohio stands out as a prime destination for both adrenaline-fueled action and deeply moving drama. The state’s urban centers and scenic vistas have set the stage for everything from explosive superhero battles to intimate, character-driven stories.

Blockbusters like The Avengers have transformed downtown Cleveland into a playground for superhero action, with car chases, stunts, and special effects. (Yep, Captain America has been here twice!)

Directors such as George Clooney and Barry Levinson have chosen Ohio for its authentic atmosphere and rich storytelling potential, capturing everything from sweeping romance to gritty drama..

Why Visit Bellefontaine?

With Union County hitting theaters in 2026, Bellefontaine’s about to be the place to be for movie buffs. There’s talk of screenings at the Holland Theatre or even Ohio film festivals.

But even if you’re not here for the movie, this town’s got small-town charm that's magnetic—historic sites, great food, and much, much more. Bellefontaine is becoming a bigger draw, whether you’re a film buff, a couple looking for a weekend trip, or a local from Logan or Union County.

Downtown Bellefontaine: Where It All Happens

Bellefontaine has America’s first concrete street—a little piece of history perfect for your Instagram reel. Even more, you’ll see a lineup of shops, cafes, and restaurants that are catching the attention of more and more daytrippers as time goes on. Pop into Brewfontaine for a craft beer (maybe where Poulter sat!) or grab a coffee at Sweet Aromas.
