Some of these spots might show up in Union County How fun would it be to watch the movie and see locations tied to personal memories?

The Holland Theatre: Check It Out!

The Holland Theatre is a treasure. Built in 1931, it has this incredible Art Deco style and hosts concerts, plays, and community events.

Stepping inside feels like a trip back in time. (Once you get here, you’ll completely understand.)

The Holland’s history adds to its allure. Designed by Peter Hulsken, it was one of the first theaters in Ohio to feature “talkies” (sound films), making it a fitting venue for Union County’s premiere. The theater’s restoration in the 2000s preserved its original murals and chandeliers.

In 2026, there could be special events, like cast Q&As or film history talks, drawing film fans from across Ohio.

Ohio’s Film Industry: Behind the Scenes

What makes Ohio such a magnet for filmmakers? It’s not just the scenery—it’s the thriving film industry working behind the scenes.

Thanks to the Ohio Motion Picture Tax Credit, productions can save big on expenses, making the Buckeye State an attractive choice for everything from indie projects to Hollywood blockbusters. This incentive has drawn major films like The Ides of March (2011), directed by George Clooney and filmed in Cincinnati.

We also mentioned Spider-Man, Captain America, and the Avengers!

Ohio’s film scene is also fueled by a passionate creative community and a calendar packed with film festivals. The Cleveland International Film Festival, for example, brings together filmmakers and fans from around the globe, celebrating independent films.

Ohio Film History

Ohio’s relationship with the silver screen stretches back over a century, making it one of the Midwest’s most storied film destinations.

The journey began way back in 1905 with The Night Before Christmas, a short film shot in Cleveland that marked the state’s first foray into movie-making.

As Hollywood’s influence grew, so did Ohio’s reputation, with the 1930s and 1940s seeing major studios like Warner Bros. and MGM setting up shop and producing films in the Buckeye State.

Over the decades, Ohio has continued to evolve as a film hub, welcoming productions of all sizes and genres. The establishment of the Ohio Film Office in 2007 marked a new era, providing resources and support to filmmakers, which helped attract a steady stream of projects.

Today, Ohio’s film industry is thriving, with production companies, studios, and creative professionals calling cities like Cleveland and Columbus the place to be.

Economic Impact: How Film Production Shapes Ohio

Film production isn’t just about lights, camera, action—it’s an engine for Ohio’s economy. So any Buckeye resident should be grateful when filmmakers come knocking. 

Let’s put it into dollars and cents.

Every year, movies filmed in Ohio generate millions in revenue, create jobs, and boost local businesses. According to the Ohio Film Office, the industry contributed over $100 million to the state’s economy in 2020 alone, supporting more than 1,000 jobs and energizing communities from Cleveland to Cincinnati.

$100 million!

Thank the Ohio film tax credit, introduced in 2009, which offers incentives that attract both indie filmmakers and major studios.

Shopping Local Treasures

For Bellefontaine, which may see future productions, the area’s shops are a haven for unique finds. The Nest 1896 has cool, unique gifts, from handcrafted candles to unique crafts, perfect for souvenirs. Meanwhile, The Olde Mint Antiques is a treasure trove for vintage enthusiasts.

Why Bellefontaine Stands Out

Compared to big cities like Cleveland or Cincinnati, Bellefontaine’s quieter, more personal, and welcoming feel makes it a unique destination. While films like A Christmas Story and The Deer Hunter made landmarks like the Ohio State Reformatory famous, Union County puts Bellefontaine’s heart and soul on full display.

Getting Around Bellefontaine

Bellefontaine’s compact and super walkable layout, especially in the downtown area, makes exploring easy and enjoyable.

Just park near Main Street and easily wander to charming shops, the historic Holland Theatre, and local hotspots like Brewfontaine on foot. If you’re heading to scenic spots like Campbell Hill or hitting the slopes at Mad River Mountain, you’ll need a car, of course.

But it’s just a short and scenic drive away. The Logan County tourism website provides handy information to make planning easy.

(And what doesn’t like easy?)

Curious about tracking down Union County filming locations? Just ask a friendly local—they’ll happily point you to the exact spots where Will Poulter was filmed during his time filming in Ohio.

Where to Stay in Town

When it comes to lodging, Bellefontaine offers a variety of options to suit every traveler’s needs.

The Cobblestone Hotel & Suites provides a straightforward and comfortable stay with modern amenities, while the Loft Above is a charming B&B offers a cozy but luxury feel with personalized touches.

For those traveling with friends or family, Airbnbs are a fantastic choice, offering space and flexibility for groups. Staying near the downtown area puts you just steps away from Union County’s iconic filming spots, making it ideal for Ohio movie tourism enthusiasts.

Pairing Your Visit with Film Festivals

If you’re a passionate movie buff, consider pairing your Bellefontaine visit with a trip to one of Ohio’s renowned film festivals in 2026. Ever been to one? If not, they're worth checking out, especially if you're looking for something different than typical Hollywood fare.

Prestigious festivals in nearby Columbus or Cleveland might feature screenings of Union County, offering a chance to see the film surrounded by fellow cinephiles. These events are just a short drive from Bellefontaine, making it easy to combine them with your trip, giving you the full cinematic experience.

Why You’ll Fall for Bellefontaine

Bellefontaine’s got a unique energy that’s hard to resist. Just ask the growing number of daytrippers who arrive in town. Many visitors find themselves falling in love with the town’s charm and planning return visits.

(Who can blame them?)

That's a Wrap!

Bellefontaine, Ohio, is stepping into the spotlight with Union County, a powerful film that’s sure to impact you on an emotional level. As one of the latest additions to movies filmed in Ohio, it stands shoulder-to-shoulder with timeless classics like films A Christmas Story and The Deer Hunter.

Walk through the very streets where Will Poulter filmed, imbibe a craft beer at Brewfontaine, or catch a show at the historic Holland Theatre.

Many movies filmed in Ohio explore family relationships, including the roles of father, daughter, husband, and wife, and how these themes resonate with audiences. Of course, there's also action, romance, superheroes, thoughtful dramas, boy-meets-girl comedies, and so much more. Stories include everything from college students to Christmas, from 1994's The Shawshank Redemption to A Christmas Story (1983)

... or 2026's Union County.

As the years go by, more and more stories will be told, right here in Buckeye State. Do you want to be a part of cinematic history?

 A trip to Bellefontaine is a great start.

item.title: Proof.: Empowering Aspiring Entrepreneurs in Downtown Bellefontaine
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
If you’re an entrepreneur, Bellefontaine has a huge opportunity for you. And if you love to shop and discover new offerings, well … we have an opportunity for you too.

Introducing … Proof.

Proof. is a modern pop-up retail space in downtown Bellefontaine designed to empower aspiring entrepreneurs. Proof. offers a low-risk, move-in-ready storefront where dreamers can test their business ideas, showcase their products, and build momentum in a supportive environment.

Found on Rainbow Row Bellefontaine, Proof. is a launchpad for bold ideas and a catalyst for local economic growth. Keep reading to learn what makes Proof unique, how it supports new business owners, why its location is ideal, and how it’s poised to shape the future of entrepreneurship in small towns. To paraphrase Ron Burgundy, “It’s kind of a big deal.”

The Vision Behind Proof

The concept of Proof. was born from a simple observation: many aspiring entrepreneurs in Bellefontaine have the passion and creativity to start a business but lack the resources or opportunities to take that first step.

Traditional retail spaces often require long-term leases, significant upfront investments, and a leap of faith that can feel overwhelming for someone testing a new idea. And those hurdles can cause someone who might otherwise have been successful to turn back and put their great business ideas off to the side.

Proof was created to bridge that gap, offering a flexible, low-barrier platform where entrepreneurs can experiment, learn, and grow without the fear of financial ruin. By providing a fully equipped storefront, built-in resources, and a prime location, Proof sets businesses up for success from day one.

What Makes Proof Unique

Proof. stands out as a game-changer for aspiring entrepreneurs because it eliminates the traditional hurdles of starting a brick-and-mortar business. Here’s what makes it special:

Move-In-Ready Storefront

Every Proof. lease comes with a fully equipped space that’s ready to go. This includes essential utilities (gas, electric, water, sewage, and trash), high-speed Wi-Fi, and display fixtures tailored to retail needs. Entrepreneurs don’t need to worry about costly build-outs, renovations, or logistical setup—they can focus entirely on their products, services, or creative concepts. This turnkey approach allows tenants to start selling or showcasing their ideas from the moment they move in. It may sound too good to be true, but it’s true. Very, very true.

Flexible Lease Terms

Unlike traditional retail spaces that often require year-long commitments, Proof. offers short-term leases of one, two, or three months. This flexibility is ideal for entrepreneurs who want to test their ideas without long-term financial risk. The standard monthly rent is $1,295, with a modest increase to $1,595 during the high-traffic retail months of November and December, when downtown foot traffic peaks. These short-term options give businesses the freedom to experiment, adapt, and refine their approach based on real-world feedback.

Built-In Visibility

Every Proof. tenant receives a welcome spotlight on Proof.’s social media platforms, an automatic shot in the arm for brand awareness. This built-in exposure is especially valuable for new businesses looking to establish a presence in the Bellefontaine community. By leveraging Proof’s online reach, tenants can connect with potential customers before they even open their doors.

Optional Marketing Package

For entrepreneurs who want to amplify their brand, Proof. offers an optional marketing package. This includes professional welcome photos to showcase the business, a custom press release to announce the launch, signage support to create a polished storefront, and access to curated training videos. These videos cover essential topics like branding, merchandising, and marketing strategies tailored to small retail success, giving tenants the tools they need to stand out in a competitive market. This crash course in marketing can help make the difference entrepreneurs need to thrive.

Community Integration

Located in Rainbow Row Bellefontaine, tenants benefit from being surrounded by local favorites like the craft emporium, Wild Mushroom Tattoos, Don’s Diner, The Syndicate, and City Sweets & Creamery. This well-travelled block attracts a diverse mix of shoppers, diners, and visitors year-round, providing tenants with a built-in customer base and opportunities for collaboration with neighboring businesses.

Why Rainbow Row Is an Asset

Inspired by Charleston’s iconic Rainbow Row, this colorful block is the heart of Downtown Bellefontaine. With its array of storefronts, busy eateries, and steady stream of foot traffic, Rainbow Row is a destination for both locals and tourists.

Rainbow Row’s walkability and appeal draw a diverse crowd, from families enjoying a weekend stroll to professionals grabbing lunch at nearby restaurants. This built-in foot traffic ensures that Proof. tenants have a steady flow of potential customers passing by their storefront, whether it’s a quiet weekday or a bustling festival weekend. The block’s unique character also makes it a hotspot for Instagram-worthy moments, allowing tenants to leverage social media to amplify their brand. Being part of Rainbow Row means Proof. tenants are embedded in a supportive community of businesses and customers.

This creates a kind of natural synergy, where new businesses can thrive alongside established favorites. And shoppers looking for something new will turn up happily.

Who Can Benefit from Proof.?

Proof. is designed to support a wide range of entrepreneurs and business ideas. Whether you’re a seasoned professional testing a new concept or a first-time business owner with a passion project, Proof. provides the tools and environment you need to succeed. Some examples of businesses that could thrive at Proof. include:

  • Retail Boutiques: Clothing, accessories, home decor, or specialty goods.
  • Artisans and Makers: Handmade jewelry, pottery, candles, or other crafts.
  • Food and Beverage: Pop-up bakeries, specialty snacks, coffee shops, or dessert concepts.
  • Creative Services: Photography studios, design consultancies, art galleries, or workshop spaces.
  • Innovative Concepts: Unique products or services looking to test market demand, such as subscription box previews or niche retail ideas. As Proof prepares for its grand opening, the team is actively reviewing applications from potential tenants eager to take the leap. If you’re interested in being one of the first businesses to launch at Proof, visit itsproof.com to connect with the team and learn more about the application process.

Proof.’s Impact on Bellefontaine

Proof. isn’t just about helping individual entrepreneurs—it’s about strengthening the entire Bellefontaine community. By providing a low-risk platform for new businesses, Proof. drives economic growth. Here’s how:

Driving Foot Traffic

New businesses at Proof. attract more visitors to Rainbow Row, benefiting neighboring shops, restaurants, and services. The influx of customers creates a ripple effect, boosting the entire downtown economy and making Rainbow Row an even more appealing destination.

Generating Local Buzz

The launch of each Proof. tenant generates excitement, putting Bellefontaine on the map as a hub for unique shopping and dining experiences. The social media spotlight and optional marketing package help amplify this buzz.

Adding Economic Diversity

Proof. encourages a variety of businesses to test their ideas, from retail to services to creative concepts. This diversity enriches the downtown retail landscape, offering residents and visitors a wider range of experiences and products to explore.

Creating Opportunities

As businesses grow at Proof., they have the potential to create jobs, expand to permanent locations, and contribute to the local economy. By lowering the barriers to entry,

The Future of Proof.

While Proof. is starting in Bellefontaine, its vision extends further. The team behind Proof. sees it as a scalable model that can transform entrepreneurship in communities across the country. Every small town deserves a space where dreamers can take risks, test ideas, and build successful businesses without the sometimes-paralyzing fear of failure. The long-term goal is to expand Proof. to other small towns, creating a network of pop-up retail spaces that empower entrepreneurs and revitalize downtowns.

Bellefontaine will be the “proof” of concept, if you’ll pardon the pun.

By refining the model in Bellefontaine, the Proof. team hopes to create a blueprint for how communities can foster economic growth, support local talent, and celebrate bold ideas that give the public what they want. In the immediate future, Proof. is focused on its grand opening and welcoming its first tenants. The team is excited to see the space come to life with a diverse mix of businesses, each bringing something new to Rainbow Row Bellefontaine. As these businesses launch and grow, Proof. will share their stories, showcasing the real-world impact of this innovative concept.

How to Get Involved

Proof.https://itsproof.com/ is open now, and the team is eager to welcome new tenants. If you’re an aspiring entrepreneur with a bold idea, now is the time to leap. You won’t find less risk anywhere else. Visit itsproof.com to learn more about leasing options, submit an application, or connect with the Proof. team for more details. For Bellefontaine residents and visitors, keep an eye on Rainbow Row this summer as Proof. brings new businesses and fresh energy to the downtown scene. This is perfect for anyone looking for new experiences.

A New Chapter for Bellefontaine

With its move-in-ready storefront, flexible leases, and supportive resources, Proof. is breaking down the barriers to starting a business and giving dreamers the tools they need to succeed. As Proof. prepares to launch in Downtown Bellefontaine’s Rainbow Row, it’s poised to become a catalyst for change, fostering a new generation of businesses and strengthening the community. One last question:

What are you waiting for?

item.title: Bellefontaine, Ohio and Football: Our First-Ever Tailgate Takeover is Almost Here
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Once the first kickoff of the football season flies through the air, you know the fall season has begun. (We don’t need to wait for the “official” first day on the calendar!)

And in downtown Bellefontaine, a brand-new seasonal tradition is coming to the heart of our community. We’re turning downtown into a game-day destination for the first-ever Downtown Tailgate Takeover on Saturday, September 6, and Sunday, September 7.

Saturday: Community, Chili, and Cheers

The weekend officially kicks off on Saturday with our Community Pep Rally.

Gather with friends, family, and neighbors to show some love for all-area schools as they get us fired up for the season ahead.

Find your spot downtown to cheer on Bellefontaine's own Tavien St. Clair, playing for the Ohio State Buckeyes!

But the fun doesn't stop there. Get your taste buds ready for a delicious Chili Cook-Off, where local cooks will go head-to-head.

Think you have the best recipe in town? Or just want to sample some amazing chili? (Who doesn’t?) This is your chance.

We’ll also have classic tailgate games and activities for all ages, from cornhole to giant Jenga, so there's something for everyone to enjoy.

And for all the car enthusiasts out there, Saturday evening will also feature a special Jeep Night, where you can check out some incredible off-road vehicles. The night will wrap up with a high-energy Pep Rally Parade, which will infuse everyone with an electric excitement for gameday.

Sunday: Football, Flavor, and Fun

Make the most of our Downtown Bar Hop!

Start at any participating bar or restaurant to grab your free Bar Hop Passport. Then, explore all of the other spots on the list.

Each stop will offer the football experience you’re looking for, complete with big screens to watch the games, team-themed specials on food and drinks, prizes, and plenty of team spirit.

It's the perfect opportunity to catch all the gameday action while exploring the best of what downtown Bellefontaine’s ever-popular food and drink scene has to offer.

Eat, Drink, and Be Merry

What does Downtown Bellefontaine have to offer? Why, we’re glad you asked?

For the beer lover, you can't miss Brewfontaine, which has been recognized as Ohio's #1 Beer Bar … multiple years in a row.

If you love craft beer, this is a must. (There’s a reason for the ongoing accolades!) It has an extensive and rotating tap list of craft beers, as well as a great selection of wine and cocktails. They also have a mouth-watering menu. Check it out now, and thank us later. (Get the Reuben!)

For a different kind of taproom experience, check out Roundhouse Depot Brewing, a local brewery that pays homage to Bellefontaine's railroad history with its own unique brews.

Another option is The Pub at the Craft Emporium. Friendly, vibes, large collection of rotating taps and if you taste something you like, grab a DORA beverage and walk around to check out all of the other fall favorites in the downtown.

End your evening at Wrock Bottom Bar, the newest establishment in town. Big TV’s with all the games, family friendly arcade and a menu full of favorite game day fare, appetizers and snacks.

Does all this talk of food make you hungry? (Us too.)

Let’s talk about the restaurants in Downtown Bellefontaine.

  • The Syndicate is the spot for a special night out. They call it "big city dining in a small, charming town," and it's totally true. You'll find gourmet food and fancy cocktails in a beautifully renovated space.
  • For an award-winning pizza, you have to try Six Hundred Downtown. Seriously, this place is world-famous. They serve up hand-spun, brick-oven pizzas that are out of this world.
  • Craving Mexican? Hit up The Flying Pepper Cantina. Their tacos and other dishes are packed with zesty flavor.
  • Looking for classic comfort food? Don's Downtown Diner and The Garage Bar + Kitchen feature everything from juicy burgers to hearty meals.
  • Dig into incredible smoked meats at 2G's Barbecue and flavorful Japanese and Chinese food at Kiyomi Sushi and Steak House.

Looking for more information on the Tailgate Takeover? Stop by the event’s Facebook page!

item.title: Top Halloween Activities in Bellefontaine, Ohio: Scavenger Hunt!
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
It's that time of year again! The leaves are beginning to change and a crisp chill is in the air. That's right, the Halloween season is already here, and with it comes the return of the Downtown Bellefontaine Pumpkin Barrel Scavenger Hunt!

This unique Halloween experience invites you to wander through the downtown area, tracking down a collection of jack-o-lantern-faced barrels, each with its own ghostly tale to tell. It’s one of the best Halloween events in Bellefontaine, Ohio, and it’s perfect for the whole family.

Get Ready for the Hunt

Your mission, should you choose to accept it, is to find these haunted barrels scattered throughout downtown. Each one is a work of art, with a glowing, carved jack-o'-lantern face and a story about the spirit that lives inside it. You'll be armed with a sheet of riddles, so channel your inner detective to match the spooky rhymes to the right pumpkin barrels.

As you explore, you'll encounter a cast of characters you won't soon forget. There's Barley Bones, the spirit of a long-lost distiller, and Pumpkin Patch Pete, a farmer who became one with his crops after a shocking lightning storm. You'll also learn about the mischievous Moonshine Mike, a moonshiner whose spirit lights up on Halloween night, and Cask-O-Lantern Carl, a barrel maker who now resides in one of his own creations.

But the stories don't stop there. Be on the lookout for The Barrel Beast, a creature whose glowing eyes and toothy grin are only seen on Halloween night. And don't forget the Whispering Barrel, a spectral guardian that shares tales of the town's past.

Hauntingly Good for the Town

Bellefontaine's spirited scavenger hunt is more than just a fun family activity; it's another way the town is celebrating local businesses and community spirit. The event is made possible by generous sponsors like BUILD, The Clean Plate Club, and Michael Eller Diamonds, who help bring this festive tradition back to the land of the living. This is a great chance to enjoy a Halloween-themed event while supporting the community.

You can also check out this promo video to get a feel for the fun: https://www.facebook.com/watch/?v=1610463803148026

Rewards and Giving Back

Completing the scavenger hunt isn't just a fun adventure; it comes with some great rewards too. Once you've successfully matched all the riddles to their barrels, you can drop off your completed sheet at the drop box located at the Main Street Marketplace. Not only will you receive a $5 discount code for a booking at Decode Zone, but you'll also be entered into a drawing for a grand prize—a fantastic gift basket full of goodies from local Downtown Bellefontaine businesses.

This year, there’s an extra reason to participate! When you drop off your completed sheet, you can also bring canned goods or other non-perishable food items to support the local food pantry. It’s a great way to get into the Halloween spirit while also helping those in need.

More Halloween Happenings in Logan County

Get ready, trunk-or-treaters! Mark your calendars for a spectacular event you won't want to miss. Join us on Saturday, October 25, 2025, for the Logan County Fright Night Fest: Car Show & Flea Market Cancer Benefit at the Logan County Fairgrounds. This is a day of fun for the entire family, with plenty of festive activities for everyone. If you're interested in bringing your vehicle to pass out treats, please reach out—the more the merrier! Plus, all of our trunk-or-treat participants are welcome to join our special Car Decorating Contest for a chance to win prizes.

For more information on these and other upcoming events, visit the Logan County History Center Facebook page.

Final Thoughts on the Haunting Season

Looking for a family-friendly Halloween activity? Start in Bellefontaine with the Pumpkin Barrel Scavenger Hunt, and then explore more of what Logan County has to offer. There's no shortage of hair-raising fun, and it's all a chance to give back to the community and support local businesses. Happy hunting!

item.title: Fall Foliage in Ohio: Visit Bellefontaine!
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
The autumn season has arrived, and that means people are getting into their cars to find one thing: The best fall foliage in Ohio. If you love the fiery hues and breathtaking views the season has to offer, a road trip centered on Zanesfield, Indian Lake, and Bellefontaine is your ideal place to visit and witness the leaves changing color.

Trust us. Then thank us.

This region offers unparalleled views of the changing leaves and an abundance of great places to explore among the glorious trees once your leaf peeping is done.

Fall Foliage in Ohio: Visit Bellefontaine!

When can you expect fall colors in Ohio? The shift from green to a tapestry of red, yellow, and orange typically begins in late September and continues through early November. The best times for peak color in the Central Ohio area, which includes Zanesfield and Bellefontaine, are usually the middle of October, particularly the third week of October.

This is when the red maples and pin oak trees are at their most eye-catching, creating brilliant colors that give you the autumn season feeling you're looking for. Sunny days followed by cool nights are the weather combination that brings out the most intense fall colors. This perfect weather is often accompanied by a gorgeous blue sky.

Did you know that the Ohio Department of Natural Resources tracks the leaf change progression? Use that to plan your trip to the Bellefontaine area.

Maybe you're looking to update your Instagram, or perhaps you just want to park your car, hop out, and drink in the beautiful views. (Or both!) Either way, we have the ideal spot if you're looking to check out autumn leaves here in the Buckeye State!

The Best Places to Watch the Leaves Changing Color: Zanesfield, Indian Lake, and Bellefontaine

For a deeply rewarding fall foliage experience, focus your road trip on the rolling hills of Logan County. Zanesfield, as one of the best areas, sits on some of the highest ground in the region, providing excellent vantage points to see the entire landscape ablaze with yellow, orange, and red fall colors.

Did you know the elevation difference helps create a layered effect as the leaves begin to change? We're telling you, it makes for incredible scenery.

A simple drive through this area is a feast for the eyes, offering a tranquil alternative to crowded state park destinations like the famously beautiful (but often jammed) Hocking Hills State Park. Bellefontaine offers a more peaceful, yet equally stunning, experience with its dense concentration of colorful trees.

Just a short drive from Zanesfield, Indian Lake offers a unique perspective on the fall season. The water acts as a mirror, reflecting the fall foliage of the surrounding trees, effectively doubling the visual impact of the changing leaves.

It's like a living postcard. You have to see it to believe it.

The park area of Indian Lake State Park (especially near the pool) is a great spot to take a walk or just sit and admire the reflection of the yellow and orange hues. Plus, many of the paths are easily accessible, including options that are wheelchair accessible. This lake visit is a perfect place to enjoy nature’s artwork with the entire family, including the kids.

Sure, your kids love their screens. But can they really compete with the beauty of Mother Nature?

Dining and Downtown Delights in Bellefontaine, Ohio

After a cool day of hiking and leaf peeping in the nature-filled areas around Zanesfield and Indian Lake, surrounded by magnificent trees and fall scenery, you'll probably be hungry. And that's a good thing! You'll be able to savor the culinary scene and explore the unique culture of downtown Bellefontaine.

As countless people (and even the media) are quickly discovering, Bellefontaine offers some of the best places to eat and unique things to do, completing your fall trip ... from nature to culture. The overall beauty of the town and the surrounding nature is honestly unmatched.

For a fine dining experience, The Syndicate is the perfect place. It offers a big-city ambiance in this charming small town, featuring a menu that includes gourmet dishes such as prime steaks, seafood, and pasta. The elegant setting is ideal for a relaxing family meal after a long day of outdoor activity.

If your preference leans toward a more casual (yet award-winning experience, ahem), Brewfontaine is a must-visit. Voted the #1 Beer Bar in Ohio multiple years in a row, it features a vast selection of rotating craft beers and a menu of delectable sandwiches and appetizers.

In the mood for pizza?

Six Hundred Downtown, led by six-time world champion (yes, world champion!) Brittany Saxton, serves hand-spun, brick-oven pizzas, offering a "date night" patio.

Other options include The Flying Pepper Cantina, a brick-and-mortar spot for authentic Mexican cuisine, and Candy Craze for nostalgic sweets.

No matter what, we can tell you: This corner of Ohio knows how to satisfy a post-hike appetite.

Once you’ve had your fill of food, the downtown area of Bellefontaine is packed with unique shops.

Take a leisurely walk to explore the historic streets, including the first concrete street in America. You can find everything from antique stores to modern boutiques and toy shops.

Find over 55 vendors at The Nest 1896 and three floors of vintage goods at The Olde Mint Antiques. Queen of Hearts Fine Chocolates & Gifts offers gourmet sweets, and The Naked Goat Bath & Body specializes in natural products. The Fun Company is a great stop for toys and family events. (Your kids will thank you!)

A stop at the Holland Theatre, a beautiful Dutch-style historical theatre, is a great addition to your trip. After all, why not end the evening with an entertaining show?

Did we mention the Logan County History Center also offers a cool glimpse into the local region’s past?

Final Plan for Your Ohio Fall Visit

When to Go: The third week of October is the prime week for peak color in the Bellefontaine region, but October is fantastic overall for fall activities.

Where to See Fall Colors: Start your day in Zanesfield for elevated views of the rolling hills and all the colorful trees. Then, head to Indian Lake State Park near the pool area to see the stunning reflections of the leaf color on the water.

Post-Foliage: Visit Bellefontaine for dinner at The Syndicate, Brewfontaine, or 2 G's Barbecue, and browse the unique shops downtown.

As the yellow, orange, and red leaves change color around this central part of Ohio, you simply cannot miss this chance for a perfect road trip. This is the best time to see some of the most beautiful and easily accessible fall colors in Ohio. Don't wait until early November; the sheer beauty and abundance of trees make this a spectacular fall destination.

So, what are you waiting for? Start planning your unforgettable fall family adventure now! Your future self, with all those memories and photos, will thank you!

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Zanesfield, Indian Lake, and Bellefontaine are ideal places to visit and witness the leaves changing colors!

item.title: Christmas Stuff to Do in Ohio: Carriages and Carols!
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
November has arrived, bringing the holiday season into focus. You're ready to make memories and explore new places. So, naturally, you turn to the internet …

Looking for Christmas Stuff to Do in Ohio?

Check Out Bellefontaine's "Carriages & Carols"

Is it really Christmas without a sleigh ride? We're talking about the kind of genuine, horse-drawn experience that belongs on a vintage postcard. The kind you think about when listening to Christmas carols. The kind you see in movies. You know what we're talking about ...

Welcome to Downtown Bellefontaine, where more and more people are heading this holiday season and the rising star vying to become Ohio's Christmas Capital.

Check out "Carriages and Carols" in Bellefontaine!

This isn't just one of the many holiday events scattered across the state, like a crowded, chaotic winter village or a long drive to a major metropolitan area. This is a moment of pure holiday spirit ... the peak, must-do, utterly romantic Christmas experience that will define your holiday season.

It's the perfect, authentic tradition for the whole family.

The only catch? The demand is huge. And there's a reason for that.

The Crucial Mission: Securing Your Carriage Ride Tickets

The horse-drawn carriage rides during Carriages & Carols are the jewel of our holiday season, and they sell out fast. Here are some quick tips to get you all set up for fun:

Phase 1: Online Booking (The Gold Standard)

Most of the tickets are sold in advance. Historically, the event spans three days, such as a weekend of December 13th, 14th, and 15th, or a similar weekend around mid-December. Keep an eye on the Downtown Bellefontaine Partnership website and social media starting in THIS MONTH!

Specifically, pay attention to announcements around November 29 for the start of the primary holiday season events.

  • When to Act: Look for the official ticket release date. Mark it on your calendar, set an alarm, and be ready to click the moment they go live.
  • The Ticket Type: Carriages are cozy, often seating 4-6 people. If you have a large party, consider booking two separate rides immediately. This ride is the centerpiece of your Christmas Celebrations in Ohio.
  • A Word of Warning: Do not wait! Remember, other major holiday events like the Cincinnati Zoo Festival of Lights or the Toledo Zoo’s Light Displays don't have intimate, authentic horse-drawn ride in Bellefontaine that offers the nostalgia of Christmas past that is truly unique.

Phase 2: Same-Day Tickets (The High-Risk, High-Reward Strategy)

A limited number of tickets are often available same-day on a first-come, first-served basis at a central check-in point, usually near the statue on Court Avenue.

  • Arrive Early: If the rides start at 5 PM on a Friday like December 6, you need to be there well before 4 PM. Even on a Sunday, perhaps December 7, when the event starts at 3 PM, an early arrival is crucial. The line for same-day tickets will form early.
  • The Waiting Game: Bring a thermos of hot chocolate (or grab a coffee from a local business downtown), dress warmly, and be patient. The reward is a 10-minute serenade from local carolers under the beautiful holiday lights.

The Holiday Season Atmosphere

Bellefontaine is Ohio's Christmas Capital. Why? We give the community the immersive, warm, and authentic atmosphere they yearn for every year.

It's that feeling. That emotion.

We can offer it ... in SPADES.

The Sounds of the Season and Holiday Cheer

As your carriage glides along, you'll notice the air is filled with the sound of live music and caroling. That's the authentic sound of holiday cheer, and you'll feel it in your soul. You'll pass by our magnificent, brightly lit Christmas tree and hear the joyous sounds spilling out from the local businesses.

The Sights of a True Winter Wonderland

Our downtown Main Street is strung with thousands of twinkling lights. Check out our cohesive, beautiful display of holiday lights that makes the whole area glow.

  • The Christmas Tree: This is often part of our spectacular tree-lighting ceremony, which officially kicks off the holiday season. You'll smile when noticing our local artisans also have their shops decorated to the absolute nines.
  • The Details: Look closely, and you'll spot adorable life-size nutcrackers and the beautiful displays created by the local businesses that make Bellefontaine feel like a real-life set of a classic Christmas story.

Making a Full Day of Your Bellefontaine Visit: The Perfect Christmas Story

Carriages & Carols is the main event, but a whole day of holiday festivities in Bellefontaine ensures you capture the most genuine holiday spirit.

Step 1: The Holiday Shopping & Delicious Treats

Start your afternoon exploring the unique gift shop and boutiques. This is your chance to find truly unique gifts.

  • Food Focus: Grab some delicious treats and a hearty, hot food dinner at one of our phenomenal downtown restaurants. Don't forget that aforementioned Maple coffee!
  • The Local Touch: Unlike a generic, sprawling holiday market, our shops are all housed in beautifully preserved, old-fashioned buildings.

Step 2: Santa and the Tree Lighting

Throughout the holiday season, Santa makes a special appearance in downtown Bellefontaine. Check the schedule; you can often find a chance to visit Santa at the Santa Shed, where he's listening to Christmas wishes and spreading holiday cheer for the whole family.

  • The Tree Lighting: This is usually one of the first major events of the holiday season. A spectacular Christmas tree is lit up in a ceremony that draws a crowd and features carols and that wonderful feeling of Christmas anticipation.

Step 3: The Carriage Ride (The Grand Finale)

This is the moment!!

You check in for your pre-booked ride, or you successfully secure your spot in line. The gentle jingle of the harness bells, the soft crunch of the hooves, and the glow of the holiday lights ... this is the Christmas magic we promised. The celebration continues as you are serenaded by carols.

Why Bellefontaine, Ohio, is the True Christmas Capital

Sure, you have other options when it comes to Christmas stuff to do in Ohio. There is the massive Festival of Lights at the Cincinnati Zoo, the historic Roscoe Village, the light displays in Toledo Zoo, various holiday events in Northeast Ohio, including Clifton Mill's Christmas lights. But ...

... they won't give you that same, rich Christmas experience you're looking for.

If you want something that hits the spot, check out Bellefontaine. And once you experience it, you'll understand!

Here’s why Downtown Bellefontaine has the Best Christmas Celebrations

  • Many holiday events focus on being the region's largest or having over one million lights, etc. But Bellefontaine focuses on the authentic, old-fashioned holiday spirit. Our horse-drawn carriage ride is the essence of Christmas past.
  • Central Charm: You don’t need to drive between a dozen different locations. Our entire experience—the local businesses, the Christmas tree, the Santa Claus visits, the hot chocolate, and the Carriages & Carols—is all centrally located in our beautiful downtown. This makes it perfect for the whole family.
  • The Carriage Difference: You can find other holiday events with train rides or maybe even a wild wonderland theme, but the horse-drawn carriage ride through a historic, lit-up downtown, paired with live carolers? That's a unique offering!

We invite you to experience the ultimate winter wonderland this holiday season. Forget the big-city crowds and the long lines in other places. This is better.

And you'll see why.

Make this your essential destination this holiday season. It might be addictive, so once you try it, it may become locked in as a family tradition. Remember, the festivities begin with the tree lighting and continue through the Christmas season until New Year's Eve. We'll see you for the holiday events that define the season!

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Discover the best festive activities in Ohio this Christmas season. From holiday lights to unique events, find your perfect holiday adventure! Read more.

item.title: One of the Best Holiday Shows Near Columbus, Ohio: It's A Wonderful Life!
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
Are you searching for the perfect tradition to kick off your holiday season?

We wanted to share an incredible opportunity. You've probably watched It's a Wonderful Life numerous times during the Christmas season, but have you ever seen it performed live?

The historic Holland Theatre in Bellefontaine, an increasingly popular attraction here in Central Ohio, invites you to step back in time for It's A Wonderful Life: A Live Radio Play!

This beloved classic is now a fresh and engaging theatrical experience presented by Windmill Productions. Instead of the Jimmy Stewart movie you have nearly memorized, the story is reimagined as a live 1940s radio broadcast.

Actors take on multiple roles and create all the live sound effects. It’s a whimsical, festive spectacle that captures the fun of old-time radio and the timeless message of the original film.

Witness the extraordinary talents of the cast as they bring you the story of George Bailey on a fateful Christmas Eve, reminding us all of the profound impact one life can have.

Ticket Sales: Don't Miss One of Everyone's Holiday Favorites

Mark your calendars for a double dose of holiday cheer:

  • Saturday, December 6th at 7:30 PM
  • Sunday, December 7th at 2:00 PM

Both performances are at the Holland Theatre, 127 E Columbus Ave., Bellefontaine, OH 43311. This is a magical event the whole family will love!

The Saturday show starts at 7:30 PM, so arrive early to soak in the atmosphere.

Stop Searching for "Holiday Shows Columbus Ohio" and Discover What Bellefontaine Has to Offer

Columbus has the Ohio Theatre, home to the annual Columbus Symphony’s Holiday Pops and other shows. But more and more people are realizing Bellefontaine offers a deeply unique and historic setting They want something new, something to give them that "Christmas feeling" they're longing for. The Holland Theatre is Ohio’s most beautiful theatre and the only atmospheric theater in America featuring 17th-century Dutch architecture.

When you step inside, you'll see why so many people are pouring into town.

Try Something Different

The rounded ceiling is painted as an evening sky with twinkling stars, while two working windmills quietly stir the air.

This festive atmosphere you'll soak in is what makes every performance a special occasion. The Holland Theatre offers its own brand of intimate, historical magic.

The Holland’s story is one of true community engagement, similar to the story of George Bailey himself.

Fearing demolition, a group of sixth-grade students and concerned citizens launched a grassroots effort to "Save the Holland" in the late 1990s. (Kind of like how a group of concerned citizens banded together to save a certain resident of Bedford Falls.)

Thanks to this passion and a multimillion-dollar restoration completed in 2019, this stunning theater is once again providing live performances and is the place to be seen in Central Ohio. This incredible history makes seeing It’s A Wonderful Life here even more poignant.

Why see it here?

  • Timeless Story: A beautiful narrative of love, redemption, and the importance of every life. You’ll laugh and cry. This story, after all, hits you right in the heartstrings.
  • Unique Format: The live sound effects and multiple roles by the actors provide a clever, festive charm and a rare theatrical treat. It’s a great alternative for a more modern Christmas carol production that you've experienced before.
  • Historic Venue: Seeing a play in the incredible Holland Theatre is an experience in itself. From the gorgeous architecture to the incredible restoration, the theater's story of community engagement is, even by itself, completely inspiring.

Why Bellefontaine Has The Christmas Spirit You Need in Central Ohio

Bellefontaine itself is a charming destination, recognized as an extremely festive community right here in Central Ohio, rivaling some of the other celebrated "Christmassy" towns. It's the perfect spot to create a new holiday tradition away from the Columbus crowds.

Once you experience Bellefontaine's holiday charm, you'll find yourself making this your new annual tradition. If you're in Columbus, just a short drive to Bellefontaine promises an unforgettable holiday moment in one of Ohio’s most beautiful places. We're offering the ultimate, classic Christmas story in the most unique setting!

Ticket Information and Next Steps

Don't delay! Tickets are selling fast for both the Saturday (starting at 7:30 PM) and Sunday performances in December.

  • Saturday, December 6th at 7:30 PM
  • Sunday, December 7th at 2:00 PM

Whether you are looking for an alternative to what you've seen before or just want to feel the spirit of Christmas away from the Columbus city tour, the Holland Theatre is your destination. Visit the Holland Theatre website today to purchase tickets and secure your spot for a truly magical and festive night!

Don't let this opportunity pass you by. Celebrate the holiday with a classic story in a historic venue! Purchase tickets today!

Before the Show: Take the Bellefontaine City Tour!

Downtown Bellefontaine is boldly claiming the title of Ohio’s Christmas Capital by offering an immersive, authentic, and richly nostalgic holiday experience.

Unlike major metropolitan areas that host large-scale, scattered light displays, Bellefontaine focuses on creating a cozy, old-fashioned winter wonderland centered entirely within its beautifully preserved downtown. This is the genuine "Christmas charm" that many visitors yearn for.

The Ambiance: A Genuine Winter Wonderland

The historic main street sets a genuine atmosphere of holiday cheer with its cohesive, classic aesthetic:

  • Twinkling Lights: Thousands of lights glow throughout the historic main street.
  • Festive Decor: Cohesive holiday displays and festive storefronts are decorated by local artisans.
  • Centralized Charm: All unique offerings—from dining and shopping to the charming ambiance—are centralized downtown, making for a hassle-free, memory-making destination perfect for the whole family.

Must-Do Holiday Events and Traditions

Bellefontaine’s festive calendar is packed with beloved traditions, perfect for the whole family:

  • Grand Kick-Off: The season begins with a spectacular Christmas tree lighting ceremony and the beloved Hometown Christmas Parade.
  • The Crown Jewel: The popular "Carriages & Carols," a horse-drawn carriage ride, offers an intimate, quintessential Christmas experience unlike anything else in the state.
  • Family Favorites: Throughout the season, families can enjoy:
    • Visits with Santa at the Santa Shed.
    • Live music and caroling in the streets.

Experience & Shopping: The Local Difference

A big reason for Bellefontaine's growing popularity is its fantastic array of unique, independent local businesses. The town is the perfect day trip destination, easily mixing fun holiday events with great food and shopping.

They encourage visitors to get off Amazon and experience the holidays—the sights, the smells, and the memorable atmosphere—in person. A handy map is available downtown to make exploring easy.

When you're ready to shop, you'll find places that cater to all tastes, offering one-of-a-kind gifts. If you're looking for something special, be sure to explore:

  • Antiques and Vintage Finds: Check out The Olde Mint Antiques (three whole floors of books and cool curiosities) or The Nest 1896 (housing over 55 different vendors).
  • Gourmet Treats: Stop by Queen of Hearts Fine Chocolates.
  • Natural Products: Visit The Naked Goat for natural bath and body products.
  • Creative Gifts for Kids: Look for toys at The Fun Company.

If you've heard the buzz about Bellefontaine, now is the time to visit!

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: You've seen the movie, now check out the story as a live 1940s radio broadcast! Experience the story of George Bailey like never before!

item.title: Thinking About a Pop-Up Shop around Columbus, Ohio?

👉 You don’t need to take on major risk to see major results.

You just need… Proof.

Proof is a modern pop-up solution in downtown Bellefontaine that gives entrepreneurs the chance to launch, test, grow, or expand their businesses without a year-long commitment. Our turnover of successful brands has already created buzz, and our 2026 slots will fill fast.

If “New Year, New Business” is calling your name… keep reading.

Why 2026 Entrepreneurs Are Choosing Proof

Bellefontaine is known for launching and nurturing small businesses. From mentorship to community engagement to thoughtful downtown revitalization, this is a place where entrepreneurs feel backed, supported, and genuinely welcomed.

Now… Let’s Talk FOMO.

If you’re watching other brands grow, opening shops, expanding into second locations, building their following offline and you keep thinking, “Maybe I could do that?” You’re not imagining things. You could! Opportunities like this don’t sit open forever  and neither do our pop-up slots.

Your 2026 Business Starts Now

Entrepreneurs, if you want 2026 to be the year you finally launch, expand, or test your brick-and-mortar dream, now is your moment.

👉 Visit itsproof.com to learn more and secure your 2026 pop-up space.
👉 Start the New Year with a real plan, a real storefront, and a real opportunity.

Because waiting another year? That’s the one thing you definitely don’t want to do.

item.title: Best Christmas Experiences in Central Ohio: Make Time For THIS Studio!
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
For many, the Christmas season brings not just cheer, but a heavy dose of stress. (Yep, we see you nodding.) Battling crowded stores, scrolling through endless generic gift options online, and feeling the pressure to make every moment magical, often without finding the time to truly enjoy the season. Sound familiar?

We have an antidote, and it comes in three words: Make Time Studio.

Make Time Studio is a creative workshop space that specializes in custom candle pouring at their signature Candle Bar and hosts a variety of engaging, hands-on craft workshops. These include cookie decorating, stained glass, and seasonal projects.

Make Time Studio is also a go-to spot for paint parties and private celebrations. Guided paint sessions offer a fun, relaxed way for groups to create together. These areideal for birthdays, friend gatherings, and family nights out. With an instructor leading step-by-step, everyone leaves with a keepsake and a shared memory.

This year, you’re invited to trade the high-stress hustle for a visit to the warm, scented, and utterly cheerful world of Make Time Studio. The studio is transforming Christmas shopping from a chore into a joy, and holiday planning from frantic to festive.

Getting the RIGHT Gift

Experience gifts are the best kind, no returns, no wrong sizes, and all the good feels. They turn moments into memories and give people something to do, not just something to own.

The Candle Bar: Where Personalization is Poured

The custom Candle Bar offers an experience far beyond typical retail therapy. You can become an artisan of sorts, designing and pouring a totally personalized product that creates a wonderful memory or perfectly suits the recipient's style.

  • Create New Scents: Create the perfect holiday fragrance, like maybe some nostalgic combination like "Grandma’s Kitchen" or a custom blend for the Winter Solstice. They’ll light it, feel good, and think of you.
  • The Experience Itself: The time spent creating a candle is often considered the gift. Friends, couples, and family members can book a session together, leaving with a beautiful, personalized product … and a cherished memory to boot!
  • Thoughtful Details: You have the perfect opportunity here for a personalized message … or even an inside joke. Who doesn’t want to kickstart chuckles for years?

A New Christmas Experience in Central Ohio

The seasonal workshops at Make Time Studio include the following:

  • Cookie Decorating: The team provides freshly baked cookies, beautiful icing, and a festive atmosphere. Just show up and leave with magazine-worthy treats and zero cleanup. This activity is perfect for families, friends, or a memorable date night.
  • Holiday-Themed Crafts: Specialized classes, such as the Christmas Candle Workshop or winter-themed Stained Glass projects, let you create beautiful holiday decor or gifts in a relaxed, guided environment. No experience required, no worries there.

These sessions go beyond a simple class; they are opportunities for laughter, shared creation, and building memories that will be recalled and treasured for a long, long time.

But wait, there’s more …

The Ideal Holiday Gathering Spot Near Columbus

Looking for a holiday party idea that’s actually fun, easy to plan, and completely unforgettable? Make Time Studio’s Holiday Paint Parties are quickly becoming one of Central Ohio’s favorite ways to celebrate the season. Step inside a cozy, creative studio, grab a brush, and follow along with a friendly instructor as your group paints a seasonal masterpiece, with no experience needed. It’s relaxed, joyful, and guaranteed to bring out the laughter (and maybe a little friendly competition).

Paint parties are perfect for office teams, families, friend groups, birthdays, bridal showers, and bachelorette outings or anyone looking for a celebration that goes beyond dinner reservations or the usual holiday get-together. And the best part? Everyone leaves with a finished painting they created themselves, a keepsake that lasts long after the cocoa mugs are washed and the decorations come down.

Groups can also add creative upgrades like candle pouring or seasonal crafts to make their event even more memorable. Want to host a private gathering? The studio offers exclusive rentals, giving your group full access to the space, a personalized instructor-led experience, and absolutely zero setup or cleanup for you to worry about.

This holiday season, skip the stress and the same-old party routine. Book a Holiday Paint Party at Make Time Studio and give your group a celebration filled with creativity, connection, and real holiday cheer.

Make Time Studio, with its locations in Bellefontaine and Urbana, invites everyone to invest in experiences, not just things, this Christmas.

  • Stop stressing: Check out the latest calendar of events and book a spot soon … holiday workshops fill up quickly!
  • See what’s happening: View Bellefontaine Events | View Urbana Events
  • Book a Candle Pouring Session: Make a Reservation

item.title: Christmas Activities in Central Ohio: Visit Bellefontaine!
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
It’s October now, the air is crisp and the leaves are golden, but here in Downtown Bellefontaine we’re already getting excited. Why? Because the most wonderful time of the year is just around the corner! We’re not just getting ready for Christmas!

We are Ohio’s Christmas Capital. And if you’re looking to build new memories you’ll cherish for decades, put us on your holiday calendar.

If you’ve heard of a major downtown revival in Central Ohio, you’ve heard about Bellefontaine. Thanks to Small Nation and our amazing community, we’ve transformed our historic downtown. And people are flocking to it!

More Than Just Lights: The Heart of the Holiday Experience

What makes Bellefontaine Ohio’s Christmas Capital? It’s our community, our history, and a packed calendar of holiday events that turns our town into a holiday destination.

You want to feel that Christmas spirit. We get it. So many people do! Here’s how we can give it to you:

Events for All Ages

The holiday season kicks off with the big Hometown Christmas Parade and the lighting of our beautiful Courthouse Christmas Tree. This is when our downtown really starts to glow with the Christmas Spirit.

Throughout December, the cheer is nonstop. Check it out …

  • Visits with Santa: The Santa Shed is the place to be for families to meet Santa in the heart of downtown.
  • Festive Fun: From the Reindeer Dash for families to the Ugly Sweater Bar Crawl for the 21+ crowd we have something for everyone.
  • Holiday Entertainment: Our beautifully restored Holland Theatre has a lineup of holiday shows and concerts to fill the air with holiday cheer.

Shop Local for the Perfect Christmas Gifts

Are malls even a thing anymore? Not so much. What about shopping on Amazon? Is that fun? (Not so much.)

What if you could find great gifts and love the process at the same time?

Downtown Bellefontaine is home to a variety of unique local shops, offering the perfect opportunity to find special gifts for everyone on your holiday list. Whether you’re shopping for children, friends, or family, our downtown has something for everyone.

We state that with confidence because of the incredible variety. Let’s go on a quick tour, shall we?

For the little ones in your life, The Fun Company is the go-to destination for the best children’s toys and games. With a wide selection of both classic and modern options, you can find all sorts of options that will bring smiles on Christmas morning.

If you’re looking for a unique, one-of-a-kind gift, be sure to visit Nest 1896. This charming shop offers a wide range of handcrafted items and beautiful home décor that will make your presents stand out from the rest.

By the way, shopping makes us hungry. You too?

A Historic and Foodie Destination


Looking to get some grub? Dine and be merry! Peruse through these options.

  • Award-Winning Pizza: Enjoy a relaxed "date night" on the patio at Six Hundred Downtown, home to a six-time world pizza champion. You really can't go wrong with the best!

  • Ohio's Top Beer Bar: Brewfontaine, voted Ohio’s top beer bar for five years running, offers a cozy, family-friendly atmosphere and an unbeatable selection of craft beer. It's the perfect place to unwind.

  • Elevated Dining: For something a little more upscale, head to The Syndicate for local steaks, fresh seafood, craft cocktails, and an amazing weekend brunch.

  • Global Flavors: Looking to spice things up? Try the authentic Mexican cuisine at The Flying Pepper Cantina, which grew from a local food truck favorite into a fantastic brick-and-mortar spot.

Once your meal has hit the spot, there’s a lot more to do in town!

Visit Santa at the Santa Shed

A beloved tradition in Downtown Bellefontaine, the Santa Shed, presented by the Downtown Bellefontaine Partnership, is a must-visit during the holiday season.

Located in front of the historic Courthouse, this is the perfect place to bring the whole family for a magical meet and greet with Santa himself.

St. Nick will be available on Saturdays, Sundays, and select Fridays throughout the Christmas season to listen to holiday wishes, pose for photos, and spread plenty of Christmas cheer.

November 23 through December 22nd

  • Saturdays: 1 PM – 4 PM
  • Sundays: 2 PM – 4 PM
  • Friday, December 13 & 20: 5:30p-7:30p


Of course, this is only the beginning. We have a ho-ho-whole selection of events! Check out the schedule below and start your holiday planning!

Holiday Events in Downtown Bellefontaine

As the Christmas Capital of Ohio, there’s always something happening downtown.

  • November 23: Santa Shed Grand Opening
  • November 23-24: Holiday Open House
  • November 27: Thanksgiving Eve Bash with SaMax at the Syndicate
  • November 29: Black Friday in Downtown Bellefontaine
  • November 30: Small Business Saturday in Downtown Bellefontaine
  • December 1: Donuts & Wishlist featuring Santa at the Fun Company
  • December 1: Madcap Puppets: The Nutcracker at the Holland Theatre
  • December 3: WCOC Concert Band Holiday Concert at the Holland Theatre
  • December 5: Christmas Movie Trivia & Ugly Christmas Sweater Party at Brewfontaine
  • December 6: Hometown Christmas Parade
  • December 6: Holiday Night Market at the Morning Riot
  • December 7-8: Brunch with Santa at the Syndicate
  • December 7: Stained Glass Class at Triple Moon Designs
  • December 7: Reindeer Dash and A Holiday Hunt
  • December 7: Ugly Sweater Bar Crawl and After Party
  • December 7-8: Windmill Productions: A Swingin’ Christmas, The Holiday music of Tony Bennett at the Holland Theatre
  • December 11-12: Holiday Dueling Pianos at the Syndicate
  • December 12: Gift & Brews Men’s Shopping Night
  • December 13: Polar Express Family Paint & Craft Night at Triple Moon Designs
  • December 13-15: Carriages & Carols: A Downtown Christmas Celebration
  • December 14: Santa at the Morning Riot
  • December 14: Sheriff’s Office Annual Christmas Movie & Santa Visit at the Holland Theatre
  • December 14: Adult Cookie Decorating at Triple Moon Designs
  • December 15: Brush & Brunch at the Morning Riot
  • December 15: Lima Symphony Orchestra-Bells, Brass and Bows at the Holland Theatre
  • December 18: Christmas Cookie Decorating at the Photo Booth
  • December 19: Kids Cookie Decorating at Triple Moon Designs
  • December 19: Carols for a Cause at Brewfontaine
  • December 19-22: Winter Flight Candle at Triple Moon Designs
  • December 20: The Yuletide 5 at the Holland Theatre
  • December 21-22: Glow & Gift: A Luminary Shopping Experience

As the days get shorter, there's no better time to start planning your trip now. Come and see why Downtown Bellefontaine is proud to be called Ohio's Christmas Capital!

One last question: What’s the first holiday event you’ll put on your calendar: the parade or the Santa Shed?

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Looking for impactful memories this Christmas season? Visit Bellefontaine and prepare to be amazed!

item.title: Mad River Mountain's Location: Right Next Door to Bellefontaine
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
The mountain itself is located at 1000 Snow Valley Road in Zanesfield, but that's only half the story. Only a few minutes down the road in downtown Bellefontaine, you'll find a whole different kind of memorable experiences waiting for you.

A day of skiing and then heading into Bellefontaine? It's a match made in Heaven.

While Mad River Mountain is renowned for its world-class skiing, the town of Bellefontaine is rapidly becoming a fantastic place to unwind after a long day on the slopes. And if you are planning to visit for a few days on the terrain, there are plenty of great options for lodging.

Why Bellefontaine Should Be on Your Itinerary

Bellefontaine is well and truly back in the spotlight, and it's looking better than ever. In fact, over 58 historic buildings have been lovingly restored into unique shops and eateries for visitors to Mad River Mountain. The result is that more and more people are discovering this fantastic little town as the perfect place for a day trip or as a base for a multi-day skiing adventure.

It's got to the point where we're starting to see more and more regular visitors. Curious why? Come visit, and find out! It's one thing to read information here; it's quite another to experience it for yourself.

After the Ski Resort: Food & Fun

After a day of carving up the mountain on your skis, you're going to be starving. (Trust us, we know from experience!) So, here are a few of our top picks for grabbing a bite to eat and warming up in the cold at Mad River Mountain:

  • Brewfontaine: Ohio's number one beer bar! You can get a great craft pint and a fantastic panini to go with it, all in a cozy environment that's perfect for unwinding after a day of skiing. This is a great spot to hang out with friends.
  • Six Hundred Downtown: The owner is a six-time world pizza champion—you might have guessed that already, though. They make their pizzas in old-world brick ovens at 600 degrees, which gives them a taste like no other. Try the award-winning "Montana" or the world-famous garlic knots.
  • The Syndicate: There's a great story behind this place. It's a beautifully restored 1940s- newsstand-turned-restaurant, with a custom bar made from glass and a gourmet menu that's got something for everyone—from prime steaks to a huge brunch on the weekends. It makes a great date night!
  • The Flying Pepper Cantina: If you love the flavors of Mexico, then this is the place to go. They make all their dishes from scratch, using regional recipes to bring a taste of Mexico to Bellefontaine. Try the street tacos, birria, or a handmade guava margarita.
  • The Morning Riot: This place is a riot—fun, quirky, and full of color. They do the most amazing breakfast sandwiches, like the "Common Man" on an everything bagel with hollandaise. It's a great place to fuel up for the day or grab a treat after a skiing session.

Unbelievable Antique Hunting

Bellefontaine is also a strong stop for antique and vintage lovers. You have to check out these two massive antique shops in Bellefontaine:

  • The Olde Mint Antiques: This is a four-story treasure trove in the heart of the old town. You can lose yourself for hours browsing their collection of vintage furniture, rare books, and just about anything else you can think of.
  • The Nest 1896: This place is like a Christmas miracle—over 60 local vendors all under one roof. It's like a one-stop shop for vintage vinyl records, antique furniture, handmade jewelry, and just about anything else you could imagine. Have a question? You'll find someone with a smile on their face, happy to help you.

Something for Everyone

  • The Naked Goat Bath & Body: If you're looking for natural skincare products that are actually made with goat milk, then this is the place to go. Their soaps, lotions, and multi-balms are perfect for soothing dry skin after a day skiing in the wind at Mad River Mountain.
  • Queen of Hearts: This is a luxury gift shop that's just a little bit different. They've got gourmet chocolates, beautiful pottery, and all sorts of other lovely things to treat yourself to.
  • The Fun Company: Since 1983, this place has been a local favorite for toys, games, and just about every other kind of fun thing you can imagine. It's a great place to keep the kids and adults entertained for hours.

Modern Perks & Amenities

To make your visit to Mad River Mountain as easy as possible, we've also got a DORA (Designated Outdoor Refreshment Area) set up in the town. So, grab a drink from a local spot and enjoy it while you wander around the shops.

More to See & Do Around Mad River Mountain - Weekend Getaway

If you're looking to turn a day of skiing into a whole weekend, you've got plenty of options to weigh in on when you're looking to book some lodging nearby. Here's what's on the agenda:

  • Axe Quacks: Check out NEW duckpin bowling right in Downtown Bellefontaine.  
  • Make Time: Make time for new creations including pouring your own candle.
  • The Holland Theatre: Catch a show in this beautifully restored historic loc
  • Putt & Play: Golf in the cold with their indoor gold simulators and putt putt course.

Quick Tips for Making the Most of Your Mad River Mountain Visit

  • Winter Bonus: Be aware that Bellefontaine gets pretty crazy in the winter, so make sure to get your dinner reservations and lodging locked down as early in the week as you can. Why not get things moving today?
  • Before You Go: Give the official page a look to find out what the snow's looking like and get up to speed on trail conditions.
  • Manage Your Account: Go ahead and log in to grab that season pass or book a lesson in advance—easy peasy.
  • Rent, Refine, Re-Ski: Don't have your own gear? No problem! Mad River Mountain has what you need. They offer all sorts of rentals, plus lessons to get you skiing in no time.

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Planning a trip to Mad River Mountain? Discover the best places to eat, shop, and relax in nearby Bellefontaine, Ohio!

item.title: The Doors in Concert: A Special Bellefontaine Tribute
item.dc:creator: admin_nexa
item.content:encoded:
Many Baby Boomers are nostalgic for an era that no longer exists. And even the younger generations have embraced the music of the 1960s. Today, they have a chance to step back in time.

Mark your calendars for Thursday, February 26th. That’s when The Ultimate Doors hit the Holland Theatre stage at 7:30 PM.

For those who grew up with a record player spinning in the corner and a stack of vinyl on the floor, this is an opportunity to revisit the raw, poetic, and slightly wild energy of The Doors.

The Lizard King!

While the rest of the world was busy with "flower power", Jim Morrison, Ray Manzarek, Robby Krieger, and John Densmore were pushing the boundaries of the decade, exploring the darker side, if you will.

Morrison, the "Lizard King", brought a level of theatrical energy to the stage that had never been seen before. They blended blues, jazz, and classical influences into something completely new.

Think of the haunting "The End", the driving rain of "Riders on the Storm", or the raw, unadulterated rock energy of "L.A. Woman". They were rock philosophers with electric guitars, and their music still has the power to move us today, 50 years after it was first released.

The Closest You’ll Get

To fill the shoes of rock royalty is no easy feat, but The Ultimate Doors built a reputation as one of the most powerful tribute acts in the country. Critics and fans alike have praised the group as "uncannily true to the original" and "the closest you'll get to seeing the Doors live today."

Do you regret never having seen The Doors live when they toured? This experience will bring you close to that experience.

They use period-correct instruments and vintage gear to recreate the unmistakable tones so many people remember. To get that sound right, you need the specific roar of the Gibson G-101 organ and the particular snap of the percussion.

Meet the talented lineup behind The Ultimate Doors:

  • Darrian Kenney is the man with the deep, brooding Morrison voice.
  • Branden Eifrid is the maestro of the organ, recreating Manzarek's legendary keyboard bass lines.
  • Todd Huffman is the guitarist with the intricate finger-picked style of the original Doors.
  • Jordyn Huffman completes the quartet on percussion, bringing that signature Doors swing.

Experience the Magic of the Holland

The music will be incredible, and the setting is what makes a night at the Holland fully unforgettable. If you haven't been lately, you're in for a treat. The Holland is a local treasure and the only "atmospheric" theatre in the United States designed with 17th-century Dutch architecture.

Step inside and you'll be transported to a quaint Dutch village square under a twilight sky. You'll see:

  • Twinkling stars and moving clouds projected across the rounded ceiling.
  • Actual working windmills that quietly stir the air on either side of the stage.
  • Detailed brick facades and window boxes filled with tulips bring the history of the building to life.

This theatre was saved from the wrecking ball by a group of local sixth-graders and dedicated neighbors, and after a multimillion-dollar restoration in 2019, it's truly a big part of the soul of downtown Bellefontaine.

Plan Your Evening in Bellefontaine

The show kicks off at 7:30 PM, which gives you plenty of time to enjoy the local scene. Why not make a full evening of it? You can grab a pre-show dinner at The Syndicate, where you can enjoy a great meal in a beautifully restored historic space, or head over to Brewfontaine for a craft beer and one of the best sandwiches in the county.

Just a few quick reminders:

  • Tickets: All sales are final (no refunds or exchanges), so make sure to double-check your plans.
  • Location: 127 E Columbus Ave, Bellefontaine, OH 43311.

It's going to be one of those nights where the music is loud, the atmosphere is beautiful, and the memories come flooding back. Break on through!

wp:postmeta.wp:meta_value: Do you love listening to The Doors? Wish you could see them in concert? A very special opportunity at Holland Theatre awaits!

item.title: Axe Quacks Throwing & Bowling Arcade: A Must-Visit Attraction in Downtown Bellefontaine
item.dc:creator: admin_sn
item.content:encoded:
A familiar historic building in Downtown Bellefontaine has entered an exciting new chapter. Located at 139 S. Main Street, Axe Quacks Throwing & Bowling Arcade is quickly becoming one of Ohio’s most talked-about entertainment destinations, blending axe throwing, arcade games, and interactive duckpin bowling into one family-friendly venue.

The building itself has a long and storied past. Once home to the Belle Theatre, later a Sears department store, and eventually a flooring shop, the space has always played a role in downtown life. Today, it has been reimagined as a place where competition, connection, and fun come together under one roof.

Owned and operated by local military veteran Rick Sheriff and his wife Tami, Axe Quacks reflects both small-town roots and a bold vision for modern entertainment. Rick, who previously worked at the venue, saw an opportunity to expand its appeal beyond axe throwing alone.

“We wanted to create a place where everyone could walk in and find something they enjoy,” said Rick Sheriff, owner of Axe Quacks. “Axe throwing is still a big part of who we are, but adding arcade games and duckpin bowling lets families, couples, and groups all share the experience.”

Today’s Axe Quacks offers a lineup that keeps guests coming back. Visitors can try their hand at modern axe-throwing bays with on-site training support, roll frames on four premium interactive duckpin bowling lanes, or jump into classic fun with skee-ball, darts, pinball machines, and vintage-style arcade favorites. The duckpin bowling lanes, in particular, have become a standout feature, with immersive scoring, animated displays, and themed games that appeal to all ages.

The venue also serves snacks and beverages, including adult drink options, making it easy to settle in after exploring downtown’s shops, coffee houses, and restaurants. Carry-in food is welcome for parties, and private rooms are available for birthdays, office outings, youth groups, and reunions.

“Projects like Axe Quacks show what’s possible when local ownership, historic spaces, and smart reinvestment come together,” said Jason Duff, Founder and CEO of Small Nation. “This building has served Bellefontaine for generations, and now it’s doing that again in a way that draws families, visitors, and energy back downtown.”

For visitors, Axe Quacks adds another reason to plan a trip to Bellefontaine.

“Axe Quacks gives visitors something unexpected and memorable,” said Jamison Thornton, Director of the Logan County Visitors Bureau. “It’s perfect for a weekend stop, a rainy-day activity, or a group outing that pairs easily with everything else downtown has to offer.”

Whether you’re planning a date night, a family day trip, or a group celebration, Axe Quacks delivers an experience that feels both uniquely Bellefontaine and refreshingly new.

Plan Your Visit
Axe Quacks Throwing & Bowling Arcade is located at 139 S. Main Street in Downtown Bellefontaine, Ohio. Lane reservations and private party rooms can be booked online at www.axequacks.com, and walk-ins are always welcome. For questions or group inquiries, the team can be reached by phone at (937) 404-4055. Private parties and group events may also be scheduled outside of normal business hours by request.

Hours
Axe Quacks is open Thursdays from 4:00 to 10:00 PM, Fridays from 4:00 to 11:00 PM, Saturdays from 12:00 to 11:00 PM, and Sundays from 12:00 to 9:00 PM.
